articles.title: HE ALMOST DIDN'T GO: Why Trump Snubbed NATO for Erdogan

paragraphs.en: For months, Europeans have been planning strategies to deal with Donald Trump at this week's NATO summit. And it turns out the president almost didn't go. >> I was very disappointed with NATO. And frankly, if it weren't held in Turkey, where my friend happens to be a very strong leader, a very strong uh person, I it's possible that I wouldn't have attend. I felt I had to attend because of the fact that, you know, I know he's gone all out. > 我对北约非常失望。坦白说，如果峰会不在土耳其举行，而我的朋友恰好是一位非常强势的领导人、一个非常强势的人，我可能就不会去了。但我之所以觉得必须出席，是因为我知道他已经全力以赴了。
paragraphs.zh: 几个月来，欧洲各国一直在为应对本周北约峰会上的唐纳德·特朗普而制定策略。令人意外的是，这位总统差点就缺席了。

paragraphs.en: Turkish President Erdogan picked up on that theme of a strong leader when he talked about Trump himself. >> The fact that we are convening here today for the NATO Ankura summit and that all the leaders are arriving and have arrived and we will be executing the NATO summit tomorrow with the presence of President Trump which will be adding might and strength to our summit. And two days earlier on Independence Day, another world leader singled out Donald Trump and the role of the United States. Russian President Vladimir Putin spent 85 minutes on the phone with President Trump. And that call has more to do with reality than all the European posturing in Anchora.
paragraphs.zh: 土耳其总统埃尔多安在谈及特朗普本人时，呼应了“强人领袖”这一主题。>> 我们今天在此齐聚举行北约安卡拉峰会，各国领导人已陆续抵达。明天，在特朗普总统的出席下，我们将正式召开北约峰会，这将为峰会增添力量与分量。而在两天前的独立日，另一位国家领导人特意提到了唐纳德·特朗普以及美国所扮演的角色。俄罗斯总统弗拉基米尔·普京与特朗普总统通了长达85分钟的电话。与欧洲各国在安卡拉的种种姿态相比，这次通话才更贴近现实。

paragraphs.en: Erdogan and Putin understand something European leaders and Mark Carney don't. And by the end of this video, you will too. I'm Susan Kokinda and I've tracked British imperial management of American foreign policy for decades. Here's what I'm going to cover today. First, how Trump's visit to Turkey signals a new set of rules for foreign policy.
paragraphs.zh: 埃尔多安和普京明白欧洲领导人和马克·卡尼所不懂的一点。而看完这段视频，你也会明白。我是苏珊·科金达，几十年来我一直追踪研究英国对美外交政策的帝国式管理。今天我要讲的内容如下。首先，特朗普访问土耳其如何标志着外交政策新规则的确立。

paragraphs.en: Second, how Carney and the Europeans walk themselves into a dead end. And third, the importance of Putin's phone call and how it points to the end of a century of geopolitics. So, look at how Turkey greeted President Trump. First came the ginormous motorcade and mounted cavalry escort.
paragraphs.zh: 第二，卡尼与欧洲人是如何把自己逼入死胡同的。第三，普京的那通电话为何至关重要，以及它如何预示着一个世纪的地缘政治格局走向终结。那么，看看土耳其是如何迎接特朗普总统的。最先登场的是规模极其庞大的车队与骑兵护卫队。

paragraphs.en: And that was followed by President Erdogan standing with President Trump while Canon saluted the Star Spangled Banner. Now, by contrast, EU had Ursula Vanderelion arrived in what looked like a Turkish Uber and Canada's Mark Carney was greeted on the tarmac by Turkeykey's finance minister. Seeing the difference between the reception afforded to Trump and these others will make it easier to understand what really went on. Now, President Trump made some very precisely crafted statements about Turkey. >> Turkey has been a great ally for us.
paragraphs.zh: 随后，埃尔多安总统与特朗普总统并肩而立，礼炮鸣响向《星条旗永不落》致敬。相比之下，欧盟的冯德莱恩乘坐着一辆看起来像土耳其优步的车抵达，而加拿大的马克·卡尼则在停机坪上由土耳其财政部长亲自迎接。对比特朗普与其他人所受到的接待差异，将有助于我们更清楚地了解幕后究竟发生了什么。接着，特朗普总统就土耳其发表了一些措辞极为精准的声明。>> 土耳其一直是我们伟大的盟友。

paragraphs.en: Turkeykey's been Turkey could have gone on the side. They know Iran very well and they know the problems with Iran, but they've been very instrumental along with a couple of other countries of helping. They could have gotten into, you know, here's some people their relationship with Israel. They could have gotten into the fight.
paragraphs.zh: 土耳其本可以选边站。他们非常了解伊朗，也清楚与伊朗存在的问题，但他们与另外几个国家一起，在提供帮助方面发挥了至关重要的作用。考虑到他们与以色列的关系，他们本可以卷入这场冲突。

paragraphs.en: They're a very powerful military nation. They didn't do that. and he went on to put on the table the possibility of selling F-35 fighters to Turkey. That left everyone from BB Netanyahu to Mike Pence to the Wall Street Journal editorial board very unhappy. Now, Turkey has been prevented from such purchases since it went ahead back in 2017 and bought S400 surfaceto-air missile defense systems from Russia.
paragraphs.zh: 他们是一个军事实力非常强大的国家。他们并没有这么做。随后，他提出了向土耳其出售F-35战斗机的可能性。这一提议让从本雅明·内塔尼亚胡到迈克·彭斯，再到《华尔街日报》编辑委员会的各方人士都极为不满。事实上，自土耳其2017年执意向俄罗斯采购S-400地空导弹系统以来，土耳其便一直被禁止进行此类采购。

paragraphs.en: Think of that, a NATO country buying from Russia. Erdogan has also maintained contact with both Moscow and Kiev while the NATO alliance has demanded that instead he choose sides. I'm going to get back to the importance of that later. But all of this points to something important about Turkey under Erdogan and why Donald Trump decided to go to the NATO summit to see him and not Ursula Vanderion.
paragraphs.zh: 想想看，一个北约国家竟然从俄罗斯购买。埃尔多安在同时与莫斯科和基辅保持接触，而北约却要求他选边站。我稍后会再深入探讨这一点的重要性。但这一切都揭示了埃尔多安治下的土耳其所具有的一个关键特质，也说明了为何唐纳德·特朗普决定前往北约峰会与他会晤，而不是去见乌尔苏拉·冯德莱恩。

paragraphs.en: Remember Trump almost didn't come because unlike all other members of NATO, Turkey plays by its own rules. You don't have to like all of Erdogan's alliances or policies or his relationship with the Muslim Brotherhood. Donald Trump recognizes that he's dealing with a critical figure at a critical crossroads in Eurasia who is not playing by the rules of Britain's great game. And the fact that Turkey deals with both the United States and with Russia and that Erdogan didn't play the expected role in backing Iran has left the empire playing a game that's in the process of being rolled up.
paragraphs.zh: 请记住，特朗普差点没来，因为与其他所有北约成员国不同，土耳其遵循的是自己的规则。你不必认同埃尔多安的所有联盟、政策或他与穆斯林兄弟会的关系。但唐纳德·特朗普深知，他正与一位身处欧亚大陆关键十字路口的关键人物打交道，而此人并不遵循英国“大博弈”的那套规则。土耳其同时与美国和俄罗斯打交道，且埃尔多安在支持伊朗方面并未扮演预期的角色，这使得“帝国”所执掌的棋局正逐步收场。

paragraphs.en: And that's why the Europeans and Mark Carney are showing up at the NATO summit looking very much like a group of backup singers hustled up on the stage at the last minute to perform for a new superstar. Donald Trump is creating a new dynamic in the world and it's the one that the game masters don't want you to see. Well, we want you to see it and understand it, which is why you should subscribe to Prometheian Actions free newsletter. The link is in the description.
paragraphs.zh: 正因如此，欧洲各国代表与马克·卡尼现身北约峰会时，看上去简直就像一群被临时拉上台、只为给一位新晋超级巨星伴唱的歌手。唐纳德·特朗普正在重塑全球格局，而正是这种局面，是那些幕后操盘手不愿让你看清的。而我们，正是要让你看清并理解这一切。这也是为什么你应当订阅《Prometheian Actions》的免费通讯。订阅链接已放在简介中。

paragraphs.en: So, how did the Europeans prepare for this summit? On the eve of the NATO summit, the Wall Street Journal published a piece they'd been working on for months. They describe a secret marathon meeting in Brussels back in January where nearly 30 European leaders debated well past midnight about whether America, the protector of Europe, had become a threat. But the man giving instructions wasn't even in the room.
paragraphs.zh: 那么，欧洲各国是如何为这次峰会做准备的？在北约峰会前夕，《华尔街日报》发表了一篇耗时数月撰写的报道。文中描述了一月份在布鲁塞尔举行的一场秘密马拉松式会议：近30位欧洲领导人一直辩论到深夜，探讨的核心问题是，作为欧洲守护者的美国是否已演变为一种威胁。然而，下达指示的那个人甚至根本不在现场。

paragraphs.en: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney, the central banker's central banker, had been messaging Europe's leaders from a British phone number left over from his London days, insisting that Europe had to deal with the fact that the old America isn't coming back. And that article even reports that MI6, Britain's intelligence service, has been providing profiles on how to manage Donald Trump. Now, the net result of all of this is that the Europeans have marched into the NATO summit with a splendid new defense posture unveiled by EU head Ursula Vanderion and NATO general secretary Mark Rut. They released it in a co-signed article in the London Economist of course and then in a press conference in Anchora.
paragraphs.zh: 加拿大总理马克·卡尼，这位“央行家的央行家”，一直使用伦敦任职期间留下的英国电话号码向欧洲领导人发信息，坚持认为欧洲必须面对“旧美国”不会回归的现实。该文章甚至报道称，英国情报机构军情六处（MI6）一直在提供关于如何与唐纳德·特朗普打交道的分析报告。如今，所有这些举措的最终结果是，欧洲领导人带着崭新的防御姿态亮相北约峰会，该姿态由欧盟委员会主席乌尔苏拉·范德莱恩和北约秘书长马克·吕特共同公布。他们当然先在伦敦《经济学人》杂志上联合署名发表了相关文章，随后又在安卡拉的新闻发布会上进行了说明。

paragraphs.en: Rout and Vandonder declared that Europe is going to stand on its own without the United States. They said the era of outsourcing Europe's defense is over. And they use the new word of the day, resiliency. And they talk about the need for a stronger European defense industry that can produce at scale and speed.
paragraphs.zh: 劳特和范登德明确表示，欧洲将不再依赖美国而独立自主。他们称，将欧洲防务外包的时代已经结束。他们还提到了当下的新热词“韧性”。此外，他们强调需要建设更强大的欧洲防务工业，以实现规模化与快速生产。

paragraphs.en: Now, Bloomberg's coverage of this kind of spills the beans on the real purpose of this with a headline that reads, "NATO agrees to 50 billion in defense spending to plate Trump." Maybe MI6 wrote that headline. But there's just one problem. Reality has already left Carney and the Europeans behind. And keep the real question in front of you.
paragraphs.zh: 如今，彭博社对此类事件的报道无意中透露了真实目的，其标题写道：“北约同意增加500亿美元国防开支以安抚特朗普。”这标题说不定是英国军情六处起草的。但问题只有一个：现实早已将卡尼和欧洲各国抛在身后。请始终将真正的问题摆在眼前。

paragraphs.en: What are Erdogan and Putin see that these backup singers can't? Watch what happens when the resiliency slogan meets the real world. Conservative Treehouse captured the state of the German economy with this chart showing the collapse of German industrial production. And that collapse is only going to accelerate if Volkswagen's expected July 9th announcement delivers on the threat that 100,000 jobs will be cut and four plants will be closed down.
paragraphs.zh: 埃尔多安和普京看到了什么，而这些只会附和的人却看不到？当“韧性”口号撞上现实世界，看看会发生什么。Conservative Treehouse 用这张图表揭示了德国经济的现状，图中显示德国工业生产正陷入崩溃。如果大众汽车在7月9日预计发布的声明中兑现其裁员10万人并关闭四家工厂的警告，那么这种崩溃只会进一步加速。

paragraphs.en: And Great Britain, having shut down its longest virgin steel furnaces in 2024, is now down to a single plant which is on government life support. It could soon be the only G20 country that can't make its own virgin steel. The steel, by the way, that you need for warships and submarines. And Mark Carney, who's lecturing the world about the middle powers standing on their own, has just announced a prospective 20 to30 billion submarine contract to Germany.
paragraphs.zh: 英国在2024年关闭了其最长的原生钢高炉后，目前仅剩一家工厂，且该工厂正靠政府输血维持运营。它很快可能成为唯一一个无法自主生产原生钢的G20成员国。顺便提一句，这种钢材正是建造军舰和潜艇所必需的。而马克·卡尼，这位正在向全世界宣讲中等强国应当独立自主的人，刚刚宣布了一项拟议中的价值200亿至300亿英镑的潜艇合同，将向德国采购。

paragraphs.en: So, a de-industrializing Canada is buying submarines from a de-industrializing Germany. Talk about the mouse that roared. And at this summit, Cardi also revealed what his middle power alliance actually amounts to, a bank. He launched the defense, security, and resilience, there's that word again, bank, which will be headquartered in Canada.
paragraphs.zh: 于是，一个去工业化的加拿大正从一个同样去工业化的德国购买潜艇。这真是《咆哮的老鼠》的现实写照。在此次峰会上，卡迪还揭示了他所谓的“中等强国联盟”的实质——不过是一家银行。他正式推出了“防务、安全与韧性银行”——“韧性”这个词又出现了——该银行总部将设在加拿大。

paragraphs.en: The central banker's answer to a hollowedout industrial base isn't a furnace or a shipyard. It's a lending facility. And Cardi also established a new lending facility with Ukraine. So, the financial architecture is going up, but the industry it's meant to serve is coming down.
paragraphs.zh: 央行应对工业基础空心化的对策，并非建设高炉或造船厂，而是设立信贷便利。相关方也与乌克兰设立了新的信贷便利。因此，金融架构不断扩张，但它本该服务的工业却在持续下滑。

paragraphs.en: And how did they get here? Well, they did it to themselves. Carney's globalism 2.0 still clings to the very policies, green energy, free trade, and open borders, which have already made their industries and their cultures disappear. That's the bankruptcy of Carney's middle powers.
paragraphs.zh: 他们为何会走到今天这一步？说白了，这是他们自作自受。卡尼的“全球化2.0”依然死守那些早已导致其产业与文化消亡的政策——绿色能源、自由贸易和开放边境。这正是卡尼“中等强国”战略的破产。

paragraphs.en: They announce that defense industrial production is the key to deterrence. The very week that VW announces massive layoffs and shutdowns, they're promising a war economy they no longer have the means to build. Which brings us back to the question we started with. What do Erdogan and Putin understand that Europe doesn't?
paragraphs.zh: 他们宣称，国防工业生产是威慑的关键。就在大众汽车宣布大规模裁员与停产的同一周，他们却许诺构建他们已无力打造的战时经济。这又把我们带回了最初的问题：埃尔多安和普京究竟明白什么，而欧洲却未能理解？

paragraphs.en: President Trump is building a different set of alliances with nations that are intent on their own interests and Turkey is one of those. Here's something else from that press conference. asked about the Ukraine Russia conflict. President Trump said it's going to get settled and then he pointed to Erdogan and he said >> it along very well with and we're going to get it settled and helping us get it settled as he's helping with Iran. >> So think about what's actually going on. You're watching the president restructure the geopolitical map.
paragraphs.zh: 特朗普总统正在与那些一心谋求自身利益的国家构建全新的联盟关系，土耳其便是其中之一。以下是那场新闻发布会上的另一段内容：当被问及俄乌冲突时，特朗普总统表示，该冲突必将得到解决。随后他指向埃尔多安说道：“我们相处得很融洽，我们一定会解决这个问题，他也在协助我们解决，正如他协助处理伊朗问题一样。”请大家仔细想想这背后究竟意味着什么。你正在见证总统重塑全球地缘政治版图。

paragraphs.en: Nations act in their own interest and Trump figures out how to deal with them as they are instead of everyone playing an imposed role in a world of perpetual division. And the biggest division of all, the one the entire system has been built around has been keeping Russia at arms length from the United States, which is what makes the July 4th phone call so important. Here's how Putin's spokesman Yuri Ushikov described part of that discussion. Today's conversation between the two heads of state began with President Putin congratulating Donald Trump and the American people on their landmark holiday and recalling Russia's contribution to the development of American statehood.
paragraphs.zh: 各国皆以自身利益为重，而特朗普则学会顺应各国的现实处境与之打交道，而非在一个持续分裂的世界中强求各方扮演被强加的角色。而其中最根本的分歧，也是整个体系赖以构建的核心，便是将俄罗斯与美国刻意保持距离。这正是7月4日那次通话至关重要的原因所在。以下是普京发言人尤里·乌沙科夫对此次通话部分内容的描述。两位国家元首的通话始于普京总统向唐纳德·特朗普及美国人民致以独立日祝贺，并回顾了俄罗斯对美国建国历程所作出的贡献。

paragraphs.en: In this context, the presidents noted the importance of treasuring the historical heritage which our nations share. And that shared heritage has placed Russia on America's side against the British. Starting with the Revolutionary War, it was Katherine the Great of Russia who organized the League of Arm Neutrality to break Britain's grip on the seas during the American Revolution. And when we fought the Civil War against the British supported Confederacy, it was the Russian fleet that sailed into New York and San Francisco harbors in 1863 as a warning to London to not directly intervene.
paragraphs.zh: 在此背景下，两国元首指出，珍视我们两国共享的历史遗产至关重要。正是这一共同遗产，使俄罗斯在对抗英国时站在美国一边。从独立战争时期起，正是俄国的叶卡捷琳娜大帝组建了武装中立同盟，以打破英国在美国独立战争期间对海洋的控制。而在美国内战期间，面对英国支持的南方邦联，1863年俄罗斯舰队驶入纽约和旧金山港口，正是向伦敦发出警告，切勿直接干涉。

paragraphs.en: And when Russia set out to industrialize, it was the American system by name that Russia's finance minister Sergey Vitta turned to. And that turn only deepened the British Empire's fear of Russia. It had marked Russia as a threat to its great game in Eurasia long long long before the Bolevik Revolution. And there was more to that July 4th conversation between Trump and Putin that must have had the empire in a tizzy.
paragraphs.zh: 当俄罗斯着手推进工业化时，其财政部长谢尔盖·维特正是明确点名采用了美国的体系。而这一转向只会加深大英帝国对俄罗斯的忌惮。早在布尔什维克革命很久很久很久之前，俄罗斯就已经被视作大英帝国在欧亚大陆“大博弈”中的威胁。此外，特朗普与普京在7月4日的谈话背后另有玄机，这必定让那个帝国陷入了极大的慌乱之中。

paragraphs.en: Ushikov reported that the two presidents discussed the colossal potential for mutually beneficial cooperation between the two countries and then they discussed the upcoming launch of a Russian US crew from Russia's Biconor Space Launch Center as quote a specific example proving the two powers interest in close cooperation across different fields. This is what the empire fears the most. Sovereign nations finding common ground in economic development and especially in pushing the frontiers of human knowledge. Now go back to the collapsing economies of Carney's middle powers and their increasing irrelevance.
paragraphs.zh: 乌希科夫报道称，两位总统探讨了双方极为广阔的互利合作前景，随后又谈及即将从俄罗斯拜科努尔航天发射中心发射的俄美联合乘组任务，并以此作为证明两国在不同领域密切合作意愿的具体例证。这正是帝国最恐惧的：主权国家在经济发展上找到共同点，尤其是在推动人类知识边界拓展方面。反观卡尼的中等强国，其经济正不断崩溃，日益走向边缘化。

paragraphs.en: That is what Erdogan understands. That is what Putin understands. A new world built on the American system and an American foreign policy that deals with nations as they actually are. For over a hundred years, men who weren't in the room set the rules, keep nations weak and divided, and especially keep Russia and America apart.
paragraphs.zh: 这正是埃尔多安所明白的。这正是普京所明白的。一个建立在美式体系之上、奉行正视各国真实状况的外交政策所构筑的新世界。百余年来，那些不在场的人定下规矩，使各国保持弱小与分裂，尤其将俄罗斯与美国相互对立。

paragraphs.en: This week in Anchora, you watch those rules continue to fall apart. President Trump didn't fly to a NATO summit. He flew to see a leader who plays by his own rules. Putin didn't call to threaten.

articles.title: BURIED: Obama's $850M "Library" Hides the Files on the Coup Against Trump

paragraphs.en: So, this isn't a presidential library, it's a cover-up. paragraphs.en: She didn't apologize to the black contractors who worked on the center and got stiffed by Obama and now are in bankruptcy. Most of all, she didn't apologize to the American people for Barack Obama's attempt to steal and revise an entire eight years of American presidential history. paragraphs.zh: 她未曾向参与该中心建设的非裔承包商致歉，这些人曾被奥巴马拖欠款项，如今已陷入破产境地。更为重要的是，她未曾就巴拉克·奥巴马企图窃取并篡改整整八年美国总统历史一事，向美国人民作出道歉。事实上，自赫伯特·胡佛总统以来，历任美国总统均将官方档案捐赠给联邦政府。这些文件、往来信函与决策记录均由国家档案馆妥善保管，不仅向历史学者与研究人员开放，亦由档案馆向美国公众提供查阅。

That's why the senators and congressmen who tolerated and encouraged this sedition are so freaked out now. Their dirty laundry is also about to be exposed.
paragraphs.zh: 我不接受政府任何资金。我认为记者多公开信息、发挥应有的作用是一件好事，但我希望你在个人层面上了解这一点，并且>> 比尔·波利蒂什作为代理国家情报总监的职责将是强制推进这些解密工作，接过图尔西·加巴德的接力棒。这就是为什么那些曾纵容并煽动这场叛乱的参议员和众议员现在如此惊慌失措。他们的肮脏内幕也即将曝光。

paragraphs.en: So, let's go back to where we started. Why did Barack Obama avoid creating a traditional presidential library with full public transparency about what went on in his administration? Because a presidential library belongs to history and history, real history, accessible history, the kind that can be subpoenaed and cross-referenced and contradicted is no longer something that people who built the Obama era are willing to let run free. Orwell didn't imagine a Ministry of Truth built on cleared Southside land.
paragraphs.zh: 那么，让我们回到最初的话题。为什么巴拉克·奥巴马不愿建立一座传统的总统图书馆，以对其政府运作保持完全公开透明？因为总统图书馆属于历史。而历史——真正的历史、可公开查阅的历史，那种能够被依法调取、交叉比对和质疑反驳的历史——已不再是被奥巴马时代的缔造者们愿意任其自由公开的东西。奥威尔未曾设想，真理部会建在清理平整的南区土地上。

paragraphs.en: He did imagine it funded by private equity and tech billionaires, but he absolutely understood the purpose. Obama, the Democrats, the Bush Republicans, they're all united now in trying to lie and cheat their way through the midterm elections. They hope to kill the Trump presidency in the midterms. At Promethean Action, we're dedicated to not letting them do that, to making them pay for their crimes, the ones they committed against the president and the American people.
paragraphs.zh: 他确实曾设想这笔资金由私募股权和科技亿万富翁提供，但他完全清楚其目的。奥巴马、民主党人以及布什时期的共和党人如今已联手，妄图靠谎言和舞弊挺过中期选举。他们希望在中期选举中终结特朗普的总统任期。在“普罗米修斯行动”，我们致力于阻止他们得逞，让他们为其对总统和美国人民犯下的罪行付出代价。

paragraphs.en: But, we need your help now to do that. If you haven't already subscribed to our newsletter, please do so now. This has been your Saturday update. Thanks for listening. >> Please take a moment and subscribe to our free newsletter at prometheanaction.com. > 请花一点时间，访问 prometheanaction.com 订阅我们的免费电子报。
paragraphs.zh: 但现在我们需要您的帮助才能做到这一点。如果您尚未订阅我们的电子报，请立即订阅。以上就是本期的周六更新。感谢您的收听。

articles.title: The REAL Reason Tucker Turned on Trump

paragraphs.en: Tucker Carlson featured the anti-semite Nick Fuentes in a softball interview last week that ignited a media and social media firestorm centered on the divisions in the Trump movement about Israel. Trump's critics, including many former supporters like Carlson, claim that Trump is controlled by Israel despite massive evidence to the contrary. I'm Barbara Boyd and I've seen my share of cybar ops directed at splitting up organizations in my 50 years in politics. One of the most used tools is the question, where are you going to be when your leader dies?
paragraphs.zh: 塔克·卡尔森上周对反犹太主义者尼克·富恩特斯进行了一场“放水”式的采访，引发了媒体和社交网络上的轩然大波，焦点集中在特朗普运动内部围绕以色列问题的分歧上。特朗普的批评者，包括许多前支持者如卡尔森，声称特朗普受以色列操控，尽管有大量证据表明事实并非如此。我是芭芭拉·博伊德，在50年的政治生涯中，我见过不少旨在分裂组织的网络行动。其中最常用的手段之一，就是提出这样一个问题：当你的领袖去世时，你将在哪里？

paragraphs.en: What's the plan for succession? Today, I'm going to first outline President Trump's actual foreign policy with respect to Israel and more generally. Far from listening to the neocons and Christian Zionists of the old Republican party, he is demolishing their structures, including the influence of their British masters. Then I'm going to discuss the reason for Tucker Carlson's current disorientation.
paragraphs.zh: 继任计划是什么？今天，我首先将概述特朗普总统在以色列问题以及更广泛意义上的实际外交政策。他不仅没有听取老共和党内部新保守主义者和基督教锡安主义者的意见，反而正在摧毁他们的结构，包括他们英国主子的影响力。接下来，我将探讨塔克·卡尔森当前迷失方向的原因。

paragraphs.en: But before we get started, if you like this video, please like and share it. It increases our reach. Let's start with Tulsi Gabber's declaration that Trump has ended all regime change operations which in most cases led to conflicts that never ended. Here she is discussing this at a forum in Bahrain. >> The old Washington way of thinking is something we hope is in the rear view mirror and something that has held us back for too long.
paragraphs.zh: 但在我们开始之前，如果你喜欢这个视频，请点赞并分享。这有助于扩大我们的影响力。我们先从图尔西·加巴德的声明说起，她称特朗普已终止所有政权更迭行动，而这些行动在大多数情况下都导致了从未结束的冲突。以下是她在巴林一次论坛上的发言：>> 华盛顿旧有的思维方式是我们希望已经抛在身后的东西，它已经阻碍我们太久了。

paragraphs.en: For decades, our foreign policy has been trapped in a counterproductive and endless cycle of regime change or nation building. The results, trillions spent, countless lives lost, and in many cases, a creation of greater security threats, the rise of Islamist terrorist groups like ISIS. President Trump was elected by the American people to put an end to this. And from day one, he has showed a very different way to conduct foreign policy.
paragraphs.zh: 几十年来，我们的外交政策一直陷入一种适得其反且无休止的政权更迭或国家建设的循环之中。其结果是，花费了数万亿美元，无数生命丧生，而且在许多情况下，反而制造了更大的安全威胁，催生了像“伊斯兰国”（ISIS）这样的伊斯兰恐怖主义组织。美国总统特朗普由美国人民选举产生，旨在终结这一局面。从上任第一天起，他就展现出一种截然不同的外交政策方式。

paragraphs.en: One that is pragmatic, that is deald driven, with a very realistic approach, focused on protecting American security and prosperity while engaging with the world on terms that actually make sense. During his first term, the president brokered the Abraham Accords. this historic pact between Israel, the UAE, Bahrain, Morocco, and Sudan that came from staying laser like focused on shared interests, economic prosperity, counterterrorism, technological innovation. >> During the first 100 days of Trump, the CIA's regime change outfit, the National Endowment for Democracy, was defunded and forced to go to court to get its funds unthrozen. USID, also a key player in regime change, was shut down completely. For the first time since the end of World War II, Trump's official defense strategy, written by Deputy Director of Defense Elish Colby, does not name China or Russia as the main targets of the Pentagon.
paragraphs.zh: 这是一种务实的、以交易为导向的、具有非常现实态度的方法，专注于保护美国的安全与繁荣，同时以真正合理的条件与世界各国互动。在他的第一个任期内，总统促成了《亚伯拉罕协议》。这一历史性协议涉及以色列、阿联酋、巴林、摩洛哥和苏丹，其成功源于对共同利益、经济繁荣、反恐和技术创新的激光般专注。>> 在特朗普执政的前100天里，中情局用于政权更迭的机构——国家民主基金会——被取消资金，并被迫诉诸法庭以解冻其资金。美国国际开发署（USAID）也是政权更迭的关键参与者，被彻底关闭。自第二次世界大战结束以来，特朗普的官方国防战略首次未将中国或俄罗斯列为五角大楼的主要目标。该战略由国防部副部长艾丽斯·科利撰写。

paragraphs.en: Instead, it focuses on the internal threat posed by the terrorists who crossed our border under Biden and the thriving business of the drug cartels in our nation's towns and cities. It is also focused on stopping the drug cartels in Latin America from transporting their deadly poison into the United States and most importantly shutting down their finances. The America first defense policy prioritizes building a golden dome missile defense for the United States while modernizing US nuclear forces. This throws millions of dollars already approved by Congress to fight Russia and China into limbo.
paragraphs.zh: 相反，它聚焦于由拜登政府时期越境进入美国的恐怖分子所带来的内部威胁，以及毒品卡特尔在我国城镇中蓬勃发展的非法业务。它还致力于阻止拉丁美洲的毒品卡特尔将致命毒品输入美国，最重要的是切断其资金来源。美国优先的国防政策侧重于为美国构建“金穹顶”导弹防御系统，同时现代化美国核力量。这使得国会已批准用于对抗俄罗斯和中国的数百万美元资金陷入停滞。

paragraphs.en: They can now be reallocated to support the new strategy for the homeland. That's not to mention the state of the war hawks in the Senate who have profited from the defense industrial complex's fixation on war with Russia and China. They probably will find themselves shortly without talking points, major campaign donors, and gilded invitations to dinner. Trump's successful meeting with CJ Ping last week and his continuing impressiveness about its success show that he has nothing like the old policies in mind.
paragraphs.zh: 现在，这些资源可以重新分配，以支持针对本土的新战略。更不用说参议院中那些从国防工业复合体对与俄罗斯和中国战争的执念中获益的战争鹰派了。他们很可能很快就会发现，自己既没有了谈话要点，也失去了主要竞选捐赠者，更不再有那些镀金的晚宴邀请。特朗普上周与金正恩的成功会晤，以及他持续对此次会晤成果表示赞赏，表明他心中并没有任何旧政策的影子。

paragraphs.en: Just look at this truth from yesterday. To put a fine point on it, Secretary of War Pete Hicks announced that he had opened a military to military deconliction strategy with China, a channel for back channel negotiations. So, is Trump controlled by Israel? Here's J. D. Vance discussing that absurdity. >> First of all, when the president of United States says America first, that means that he pursues the interests of Americans first.
paragraphs.zh: 看看昨天披露的这一事实。具体来说，美国战争部长皮特·希克斯宣布，他已与中国建立了一项军事对军事的冲突降级策略，即一条用于幕后谈判的渠道。那么，特朗普是否受以色列控制？以下是J.D.万斯对此荒谬说法的回应： >> 首先，当美国总统说“美国优先”时，这意味着他将首先追求美国的利益。

paragraphs.en: That is our entire foreign policy. And that doesn't mean that you're not going to have alliances, that you're not going to work with other countries from time to time. And that is what the president believes is that Israel sometimes they have similar interests to the United States and we're going to work with them in that case. Sometimes they don't have similar interest to the United States.
paragraphs.zh: 这就是我们的全部外交政策。这并不意味着你不会拥有盟友，也不意味着你不会不时与其他国家合作。总统认为，以色列有时与美国的利益相似，在这种情况下我们会与他们合作；有时他们的利益与美国的利益并不一致。

paragraphs.en: And this example, the most recent Gaza peace plan that all of us have been working on very hard for the past few weeks. The president of the United States could only get that peace deal done by actually being willing to apply leverage to the state of Israel. So when people say that Israel is somehow manipulating or controlling the president of the United States, they're not controlling this president of the United States, which is one of the reasons why would be able to have some of the success that we've had in the Middle East. >> If the dynamic wasn't already clear, the Trump administration had to deny that they were babysitting Netanyahu last week as they visited Israel to protect phase one of the peace agreement. They also made it known that Trump had threatened to cut all funding to Israel if it doesn't get on board and keep on board with the peace plan now being implemented.
paragraphs.zh: 以我们最近几周一直在全力推动的最新加沙和平计划为例，美国总统之所以能够促成这项和平协议，关键在于他愿意对以色列施加实际的压力。因此，那些声称以色列以某种方式操纵或控制美国总统的说法并不成立；以色列并未控制这位美国总统，而这正是我们在中东取得某些成功的原因之一。 >> 如果局势的动态此前尚不够清晰，那么特朗普政府上周在访问以色列、为和平协议第一阶段的实施提供保护时，不得不否认他们是在“照看”内塔尼亚胡。同时，政府也明确表示，特朗普威胁称，如果以色列不加入并持续支持正在实施的和平计划，美国将切断对以色列的所有资金援助。

paragraphs.en: Almost everyone now claiming that Trump is a tool of Netanyahu and Israel's donors have never familiarized themselves with the process that led to the Abraham Accords in his first term. Barack Ravid published a great book about it called Trump's Peace. Trump's strategy involved getting both the Israelis and the Arabs involved in a dialogue for peace centered on the region's economic development. Theatric moves like moving the capital to Jerusalem and Israel resulted in Trump becoming more popular in Israel than Netanyahu.
paragraphs.zh: 如今，几乎所有声称特朗普是内塔尼亚胡和以色列捐赠者工具的人，都未曾深入了解在其第一任期内促成《亚伯拉罕协议》的进程。巴拉克·拉维德曾出版一本关于此事的佳作，名为《特朗普的和平》。特朗普的策略是让以色列和阿拉伯各方参与以地区经济发展为核心的和平对话。诸如将美国大使馆迁至耶路撒冷等戏剧性举措，使特朗普在以色列的受欢迎程度甚至超过了内塔尼亚胡。

paragraphs.en: That's the leverage he is applying on Israel now to make the current peace plan proceed. Now, if this helped you separate the wheat from the chaff in the coverage of Trump's foreign policy, please take a moment and become a subscriber to me actions newsletter. The link is in the description. So, what's up with Tucker?
paragraphs.zh: 这就是他目前对以色列施加的杠杆，以推动当前和平计划的实施。如果这有助于你在报道特朗普外交政策的内容中分清主次，请花一点时间订阅我的“行动通讯”（Me Actions Newsletter）。链接在描述中。那么，塔克那边有什么情况呢？

paragraphs.en: Well, he let the cat out of the bag recently in a warning note for his subscribers. He titled it Banish the Serpents. Here it is. And I quote, "When people think of MAGA, they think of the president.
paragraphs.zh: 最近，他在给订阅者的一封警示信中“泄露了天机”。这封信的标题是《驱逐毒蛇》。以下是原文，我引用如下：“当人们想到‘让美国再次伟大’（MAGA）时，他们想到的是总统。”

paragraphs.en: There will always be groups that remade for against him based solely on his personality. But that's not what most of the country, and it certainly isn't young people who think that. The movement that ushered Trump back into the White House is about more than one man. It's about the ideas he represented and campaigned on 12 months ago.
paragraphs.zh: 总有一些群体仅凭他的个性就对他进行重新评价，或支持或反对。但这并不代表大多数美国人的看法，尤其不是年轻人的看法。将特朗普重新送入白宫的运动，远不止关乎他个人。它关乎他所代表并在12个月前竞选期间所倡导的理念。

paragraphs.en: What are those ideas? Tucker lists no foreign wars, closed borders, protecting US jobs, and free speech. Those are the ideas he thinks won the election. But he is dead wrong on two counts.
paragraphs.zh: 他指的是哪些理念？塔克列举了没有对外战争、关闭边境、保护美国就业以及言论自由。他认为正是这些理念赢得了选举。但他在两个方面完全错了。

paragraphs.en: The nature of MAGA and the ideas that won the election. It's not just Tucker who thinks this way. It's very common in Republican circles throughout the country who cling to past ideas. MAGA is not a list of policy points.
paragraphs.zh: MAGA的本质以及赢得选举的理念。认为如此的人并不只有塔克·卡尔森，在美国各地的共和党圈子里，这种观点非常普遍，人们依然固守过去的观念。MAGA并不是一份政策清单。

paragraphs.en: It is Trump. Full stop. It's a movement led by a truly unique historical individual. Donald Trump came away from the Butler assassination attempt with a passion to save the republic and the understanding that his life and every day now is a gift of God.
paragraphs.zh: 这就是特朗普。句号。这是一场由一位真正独特的历史人物领导的运动。在巴特勒遇刺未遂事件后，特朗普怀着拯救共和国的热情，并深刻认识到，他的生命以及当下的每一天都是上帝的恩赐。

paragraphs.en: Based on the continuous legal and physical attacks on him by the oligarchy, the people see him as a hero fighting for the forgotten men and women of this country. That spiritual quality which none of the legacy Republicans understand or have is why Trump 2028 hats freak out the Democrats, the media, and the city of London. That's why Trump standing up with Butler and saying fight is an iconic picture. That courage and passion is again what the old Republican party can't manifest.
paragraphs.zh: 鉴于寡头势力对他持续不断的法律与人身攻击，民众将他视为为这个国家被遗忘的男女争取权益的英雄。那种精神品质，是传统共和党人既不理解也不具备的，正因如此，“特朗普2028”帽子令民主党人、媒体以及伦敦金融城感到极度不安。这就是为什么特朗普与巴特勒并肩而立、高呼“战斗”的画面成为一幅标志性的场景。这种勇气与激情，同样是老派共和党人所无法展现的。

paragraphs.en: In many ways, Trump's relationship to MAGA is like Martin Luther King's relationship to the civil rights movement. In a memorial for King in Tallaladega, Alabama, Lyn Larouche discussed Dr. King's irreplaceable role. We had no replacement for Martin. Lesson number one.
paragraphs.zh: 在许多方面，特朗普与“让美国再次伟大”（MAGA）运动的关系，就像马丁·路德·金与民权运动的关系。在阿拉巴马州塔拉迪加为金博士举行的纪念活动中，林·拉鲁什谈到了金博士不可替代的作用。我们找不到马丁的替代品。第一课。

paragraphs.en: Martin was a unique personality. He was not a talented person who happened to stumble into leadership and could be easily replaced by other leaders who would learn the job and take over afterward. He had no replacement. No one in position to replace him.
paragraphs.zh: 马丁是一个独特的个性人物。他并非一个偶然涉足领导岗位、且可被其他领导者轻易接替的人，后者只需学习并接手即可。他没有继任者，也没有任何人具备接替他的条件。

paragraphs.en: Many wish to be, but they didn't have that quality. Martin was truly a man of God. Truly in a way that very few people are actually able to realize in their lifetime. It wasn't just that he was a man of God.
paragraphs.zh: 许多人渴望成为这样的人，但他们并不具备那种品质。马丁确实是一位真正属神的人，这种真实是极少有人在有生之年能够真正实现的。他不仅仅是一位属神的人。

paragraphs.en: It's that he rose to the fuller appreciation of what that meant. Obviously, the image for him was Christ and the passion and crucifixion of Jesus Christ. That was his source of strength. He lived that.
paragraphs.zh: 关键在于，他逐渐深刻领悟到这一信念的真正含义。显然，对他而言，这一意象是基督，以及耶稣基督的受难与十字架上的牺牲。这成为他力量的源泉。他以此践行自己的人生。

paragraphs.en: He had gone to the mountaintop at a point that he knew his life was threatened by powerful forces in the United States. And he said, "I will not shrink from this mission even if they kill me, just as Christ said." And I'm sure that was in Martin's mind at that point. The passion and crucifixion of Christ is the image which is the essence of Christianity. Tucker also completely misses the most important element of Trump's platform, that is the transformation of the economy to favor the forgotten men and women of this country rather than Wall Street.
paragraphs.zh: 他前往山顶，因为他深知自己的生命正受到美国强大势力的威胁。他说：“即使他们杀了我，我也不会退缩，正如基督所言。”我确信，马丁当时心中正是如此想的。基督的受难与十字架之死，是基督教的本质意象。塔克也完全忽略了特朗普竞选纲领中最重要的元素，即推动经济转型，使其惠及被遗忘的普通男女，而非华尔街。

paragraphs.en: He campaigned for and is putting into place the highly skilled jobs of the future so that one worker per household can once again provide for a family. He has also given this nation a mission once again. Create a world which consistently provides a better future for each new generation. Create a world in which the creativity of human beings is nurtured and allowed to flourish.
paragraphs.zh: 他积极倡导并着手落实未来高技能就业岗位的建设，使每个家庭中的一名劳动者能够再次支撑起整个家庭的生计。他还为这个国家重新确立了一项使命：为每一代新人持续创造更美好的未来；创造一个能够培育并让人类创造力蓬勃发展的世界。

paragraphs.en: He knows that the new frontier discoveries which will power this economy will come from his emphasis on developing fusion power, advanced nuclear power, and exploration of the moon and Mars. He's only been at it for 10 months, but the milestones are already incredible. Just go to the White House website and read through the executive orders. More than anything, most Republicans don't understand American system economics and Trump's devotion to tariffs.
paragraphs.zh: 他深知，推动这一经济增长的新前沿发现将源于他对发展聚变能、先进核能以及月球和火星探索的重视。他上任仅十个月，但取得的里程碑式成就已令人惊叹。只需访问白宫网站，阅读各项行政命令即可了解。然而，最重要的是，大多数共和党人并不理解美国的经济体系，也不了解特朗普对关税的执着。

paragraphs.en: British ideology about free markets and free trade fills their heads. Trump's tariff policies will face a big test on Wednesday when their legality is being argued before the Supreme Court. So, here's where we land. Mega isn't a checklist you can give any politician.
paragraphs.zh: 英国关于自由市场和自由贸易的意识形态充斥着他们的头脑。特朗普的关税政策将在周三面临重大考验，届时其合法性将在最高法院进行辩论。因此，我们目前的处境是：Mega 不是一份你可以交给任何政治家的检查清单。

paragraphs.en: When you hear stuff like mega is bigger than one man, recognize the play. That line is designed to unplug the movement from its engine and split the coalition before the midterms. It's the oldest scioak with a new coat of paint. It's the failure to understand Trump's approach to economics which Prometheian action is focused on curing.
paragraphs.zh: 当你听到诸如“梅加比一个人更大”之类的话时，要意识到这是精心设计的策略。这句话旨在切断运动与其核心动力之间的联系，并在中期选举前分裂联盟。这是老套的伎俩，只是换上了新的外衣。其根源在于未能理解特朗普的经济学方法，而这一方法正是普罗米修斯行动所致力于纠正的。

paragraphs.en: We are building an American where one skilled income can support a family, where our borders are secure and our streets are safe, where industry, science, and faith in the future are not talking points but daily reality. We need you, our audience, to engage on this. Go to the FISA action toolkit on the website. The link will be in the description.
paragraphs.zh: 我们正在建设一个美国，在这个美国里，一份熟练工作的收入足以养家糊口；我们的边境安全稳固，街道平安有序；工业、科学以及对未来的信念不再是政治辩论的话题，而是日常的现实。我们需要你们——我们的观众——积极参与其中。请访问网站上的FISA行动工具包，链接将在描述中提供。

paragraphs.en: There you'll find key materials to help you understand the difference between the American and British economic systems. I'll see you again when we answer your questions in our Thursday question and answer video. >> Thanks for watching. Please take a moment and subscribe to our free newsletter at prometheianaction.com.
paragraphs.zh: 在那里，您将找到关键资料，帮助您理解美国与英国经济体系之间的差异。我们将在周四的问答视频中回答您的问题，届时再见。>> 感谢您的观看。请花一点时间订阅我们在 prometheianaction.com 提供的免费通讯。

articles.title: TONY FAUCI'S KILLING FIELD: The Biolab Empire That Took Us From Wuhan to Ukraine

paragraphs.en: On his way out the door, Joe Biden issued two pardons, one for his son Hunter, one for Dr. Tony Fauci. And both of them start the clock on the exact same month, January 2014. In the last 11 days, the outgoing director of national intelligence told the cabinet has dropped two bombshells. First, about 120 secret US-funded labs in 30 countries, 40 of them in Ukraine.
paragraphs.zh: 在出门之际，乔·拜登发布了两项特赦令，分别赦免了他的儿子亨特和托尼·法奇博士。这两项特赦令的生效时间均从2014年1月开始计算。在过去11天里，即将离任的国家情报总监向内阁透露了两项重磅消息。首先，涉及30个国家中约120个由美国秘密资助的实验室，其中40个位于乌克兰。

paragraphs.en: Then, last Thursday night, the cover-up of where COVID really came from. So, here's the question we need to answer by the end of this video. Was COVID a foreseeable accident that a corrupt system just exploited? Or did they build the machinery to ensure it would happen?
paragraphs.zh: 那么，上周四晚上，关于新冠疫情真正来源的掩盖事件被曝光。所以，我们需要在这个视频结束前回答这个问题：新冠疫情是一个腐败体系所利用的可预见事故，还是他们精心构建了确保其发生的机制？

paragraphs.en: And no one would ever find out who did it. Now, these releases don't just prove a COVID cover-up. They hand you a map. A map of a dangerous bio-lab empire with one man at its center, Dr. Tony Fauci.
paragraphs.zh: 而且，永远不会有人查出是谁干的。现在，这些文件的发布不仅证明了新冠疫情存在掩盖行为，还为你提供了一张地图。一张描绘危险生物实验室帝国的地图，而这一切的核心人物就是托尼·福奇博士。

paragraphs.en: This network moved the most dangerous research on Earth beyond the reach of Congress, beyond the courts, beyond you. It gave us the worst pandemic in a century, and it may have been a significant cause of the Ukraine war. I'm Barbara Boyd. I've spent decades tracking how the imperial deep state operates, how it captures institutions, manufactures consensus, and then destroys anyone who challenges it.
paragraphs.zh: 这个网络将地球上最危险的研究置于国会、法院以及你我无法触及的范围之外。它带来了百年最严重的疫情，并可能是引发乌克兰战争的重要原因。我是芭芭拉·博伊德。数十年来，我一直追踪研究帝国深层政府如何运作，如何俘获机构、制造共识，然后摧毁任何挑战它的人。

paragraphs.en: So, I'm going to walk you through these questions. One, what did Obama actually do in 2014 that set all of this in motion? Two, how did the very man who built this network end up in charge of the investigation into his own crime? And three, the one that matters most, what was this all actually for?
paragraphs.zh: 那么，我将逐一解答这些问题。第一，奥巴马在2014年究竟做了什么，从而引发了这一系列事件？第二，正是这位构建了该网络的人，为何最终却主导了对自身罪行的调查？第三，也是最为关键的问题，这一切究竟是为了什么？

paragraphs.en: But before we begin, like and share this video. It is the single biggest thing you can do to beat the blackout on this story. Now, hold that question about pardons. It starts with 2014.
paragraphs.zh: 但在我们开始之前，请点赞并分享本视频。这是你阻止本报道被屏蔽的最重要举措。现在，先保留关于特赦的问题。这一切要从2014年说起。

paragraphs.en: Here's the first thing you need to understand. In 2014, the Obama administration banned this research, gain of function, inside the United States. But they didn't end it. They exported it the same way they exported regime change.
paragraphs.zh: 这是你需要了解的第一件事。2014年，奥巴马政府在美国境内禁止了这类“功能获得”研究，但并未彻底终止它。他们以与输出“政权更迭”相同的方式，将这种研究输出到了国外。

paragraphs.en: The same way they exported surveillance. Out of Congress's reach, out of the court's reach, out of yours. That October 2014 ban came only after the danger spilled into the press. Live anthrax shipped from a secure CDC lab to an insecure one.
paragraphs.zh: 他们以同样的方式出口监控。这些监控手段避开了国会的监管，躲过了法院的审查，也逃过了你的视线。直到2014年10月，当危险暴露于媒体之后，禁令才姗姗来迟。活体炭疽杆菌从一个安全的美国疾病控制与预防中心（CDC）实验室被运往一个安全性不足的实验室。

paragraphs.en: Smallpox vials sitting unsecured at NIH. A deadly H5N1 strain mailed to a poultry farm by mistake. Look at this. It's the 2014 USA Today article that caused Obama to move all of this overseas.
paragraphs.zh: 未妥善保管的天花病毒样本存放在美国国立卫生研究院（NIH）。致命的H5N1禽流感病毒株被误寄至一家家禽养殖场。请看这篇2014年《今日美国》的报道，正是该报道促使奥巴马政府将所有相关生物材料转移至海外。

paragraphs.en: It's no longer available on their site. But it details the bioterror dangers lurking in America's labs. And every one of the incidents it lays out was the result of gain of function research. Now, two more things happened in 2014, and that's where our story gets really interesting.
paragraphs.zh: 该网站已不再提供相关内容。但它详细披露了潜伏在美国实验室中的生物恐怖主义风险。而它所列举的每一起事件，都是功能增益研究的结果。2014年又发生了另外两件事，我们的故事由此变得愈发引人入胜。

paragraphs.en: First, Tony Fauci handed a big NIH grant to a group called EcoHealth Alliance to study bat coronaviruses at the Wuhan Institute of Virology in China. And at almost the exact same moment, the Obama and MI6-backed Maidan coup in Ukraine happened. The intent? Prepare Ukraine for a war with Russia.
paragraphs.zh: 首先，托尼·弗奇向一个名为“生态健康联盟”的组织提供了一笔巨额美国国立卫生研究院（NIH）资助，用于研究中国武汉病毒研究所的蝙蝠冠状病毒。几乎在同一时刻，由奥巴马政府和英国秘密情报局（MI6）支持的乌克兰“广场革命”政变发生了。其意图是什么？为乌克兰与俄罗斯之间的战争做准备。

paragraphs.en: Coincidence? You're about to see that it wasn't. EcoHealth is run by a British scientist named Peter Daszak. Daszak also inside a program called Predict, run out of UC Davis, funded by USAID. And Predict had a partner company you need to know about.
paragraphs.zh: 巧合吗？你即将看到，这并非巧合。EcoHealth由一位名叫彼得·达兹克的英国科学家领导。达兹克还参与了一个名为“Predict”的项目，该项目由加州大学戴维斯分校运营，并由美国国际开发署（USAID）资助。而“Predict”有一个你需要了解的合作伙伴公司。

paragraphs.en: It's called Metabiota. Metabiota had already been thrown out of its work in China and had caused a scandal in Sierra Leone for shockingly lax safety during the Ebola outbreak. So, watch this swap. EcoHealth's replacement of Metabiota in China, and then Metabiota went to Ukraine.
paragraphs.zh: 它被称为Metabiota。Metabiota此前已因在中国的工作被终止，并因在埃博拉疫情期间安全管控极为松懈而在塞拉利昂引发丑闻。接下来请看这一轮人事更替：EcoHealth接替了Metabiota在中国的工作，随后Metabiota转赴乌克兰。

paragraphs.en: Gabbard's releases show that across Ukraine, former Soviet bioweapons labs were being upgraded starting in 2014 under a Pentagon program originally meant to keep nuclear and biological material out of terrorist hands. The lead Pentagon contractor in Ukraine for this? Guess what? Metabiota.
paragraphs.zh: 加巴德的披露显示，自2014年起，在五角大楼一个旨在防止核材料和生物材料落入恐怖分子手中的计划下，乌克兰各地的前苏联生物武器实验室正在升级。负责乌克兰这一项目的主要五角大楼承包商？你猜怎么着？是Metabiota。

paragraphs.en: And on Hunter Biden's laptop is a memo to him from Metabiota's vice president. It reads, "As promised, I prepared the attached memo which provides an overview of Metabiota, our engagement in Ukraine, and how we can potentially leverage our team, networks, and concepts to assert Ukraine's cultural and economic independence from Russia and continued integration into Western society." Isn't that pretty strange language for a company that's supposed to be studying viruses? And Hunter went on to raise millions for those Ukraine biolabs. And now those two timelines start to make sense.
paragraphs.zh: 在亨特·拜登的笔记本电脑上，有一份来自Metabiota公司副总裁的备忘录。备忘录写道：“正如所承诺的，我准备了附件中的备忘录，概述了Metabiota的情况、我们在乌克兰的参与，以及我们如何可能利用我们的团队、网络和资源，推动乌克兰在文化和经济上摆脱对俄罗斯的依赖，并继续融入西方社会。”对于一个 supposedly 研究病毒的公司来说，这样的措辞难道不显得相当奇怪吗？亨特随后为这些乌克兰的生物实验室筹集了数百万美元。如今，这两条时间线开始变得合情合理。

paragraphs.en: Both of them began in 2014. Hunter's runs through January 2014 to January 2024. Fauci's runs from January 2014 right up to the minute Donald Trump was sworn in. Ask yourself, if none of this was a crime, why does the protection start the exact year the network was born?
paragraphs.zh: 这两项措施都始于2014年。亨特（Hunter）的保护期从2014年1月持续至2024年1月。福奇（Fauci）的保护期则从2014年1月开始，一直延续到唐纳德·特朗普宣誓就职的那一刻。请问，如果这一切并非犯罪，为何保护措施的起始时间恰好与该网络诞生的年份完全吻合？

paragraphs.en: If those pieces are starting to lock together for you, that's exactly what we try to do here every time we publish. You'll get a lot more of it in our free newsletter, the link's in the description. So, now you've got the machine, Fauci, Daszak, Metabiota, Wuhan, Ukraine, all of it wired together by Pentagon money and Obama's 2014 operations. And here's where it turns criminal.
paragraphs.zh: 如果这些线索开始在你脑海中拼凑起来，那正是我们每次发布内容时努力达成的效果。在我们的免费通讯中，你会看到更多相关内容，链接已在描述中提供。现在，你已经看到了整个机器：福奇、达兹克、Metabiota、武汉、乌克兰，所有这一切都由五角大楼的资金和奥巴马2014年的行动串联在一起。而这里，事情开始走向犯罪。

paragraphs.en: When the machine produced the worst pandemic in a century, the same man who built it was handed control of the investigation into how it started. Gallagher's Thursday releases do two things at once. They point to a lab origin for the virus and they document Fauci's cover-up of it. The documents show Fauci's consistent, years-long advocacy for gain-of-function research.
paragraphs.zh: 当这台机器引发了本世纪最严重的疫情时，正是当初建造它的那位人士被赋予了调查疫情起因的权力。加拉格尔周四发布的文件同时做了两件事：它们指出病毒源自实验室，并记录了福奇对此事的掩盖行为。这些文件显示，福奇多年来一贯倡导“功能获得”研究。

paragraphs.en: But they open with something stunning. A May 2020 assessment from Lawrence Livermore's Z program, the unit that evaluates advanced weapon threats, including bioweapons, for the intelligence community, a group which has access to everything. Their finding, the Wuhan lab fit every single criterion for an accidental release of an engineered coronavirus capable of infecting human beings. Now, the document is heavily redac- -ted and it points to a separate classified CIA analysis.
paragraphs.zh: 但它们以一项令人震惊的内容开篇。2020年5月，劳伦斯利弗莫尔国家实验室的Z项目发布了一份评估报告。Z项目是负责为情报界评估包括生物武器在内的先进武器威胁的单位，该单位能够获取各类信息。其结论指出，武汉实验室符合所有关于意外释放一种能够感染人类的工程化冠状病毒的标准。然而，该文件经过大量删节，并指向一份单独的、由中央情报局作出的机密分析。

paragraphs.en: Remember, it had already been reported that intercepts and satellite imagery caught unusual activity at Wuhan health activities as early as November 2019 and that lab workers had fallen ill. Then, in 2021, The Intercept got a hold of a 2018 proposal Peter Daszak had sent to DARPA. It's kind of a smoking gun. It proposed engineering exactly the kind of virus that swept through the world in 2019.
paragraphs.zh: 请记住，此前已有报道指出，早在2019年11月，截获的通信和卫星图像就已捕捉到武汉相关活动中的异常动态，且实验室工作人员曾出现患病情况。随后，在2021年，《拦截》（The Intercept）获取了彼得·达兹克（Peter Daszak）于2018年提交给美国国防高级研究计划局（DARPA）的一份提案。这堪称“铁证”。该提案提出，要人工改造出一种与2019年席卷全球的病毒完全相同的病毒。

paragraphs.en: DARPA said no. But later EcoHealth work showed the research was slated for Wuhan, a lab nowhere near safe enough for it, and may have continued under Fauci's grant anyway. Put it together and the picture is hard to escape. A foreseeable leak of an engineered virus from Fauci's own contractors.
paragraphs.zh: 美国国防部高级研究计划局（DARPA）曾予以拒绝。但后来EcoHealth联盟的研究显示，该研究原本计划安排在武汉进行，而那里的实验室远未达到安全标准，并且该项目可能仍通过福奇博士的资助得以继续。综合来看，一幅难以回避的画面浮现出来：由福奇博士自己的承包商所研发的工程化病毒，发生了一场可预见的泄漏。

paragraphs.en: Hold that word, foreseeable. We're coming back to it. Here's what Fauci did next. The bulk of these disclosures show him brought into the Biden administration's COVID origins analysis investigation as the lead expert.
paragraphs.zh: 先暂停一下“可预见”这个词，我们稍后会回到它。以下是福奇接下来所做的事情。大部分披露内容显示，他被拜登政府新冠起源分析调查聘为主导专家。

paragraphs.en: He had extended conversations with Biden, the CIA, and DNI, telling them who to call, what to cite, all of it steering toward natural origin and away from the lab. When whistleblower was flagged the obvious conflict of interest, the complaints were buried. One of those buried complaints, that Fauci had lied to Congress when he swore NIH never funded gain-of-function work at Wuhan. And the documents prove he lied to Congress a second time in 2024.
paragraphs.zh: 他与拜登、中央情报局局长以及国家情报总监进行了长时间的交谈，告诉他们该联系谁、引用哪些内容，所有这些都指向自然起源，远离实验室。当举报人被标记出明显的利益冲突时，相关投诉被掩盖了。其中一起被掩盖的投诉是，福奇在宣誓作证时声称国立卫生研究院从未资助武汉的增益功能研究，而文件证明他在2024年再次向国会撒谎。

paragraphs.en: Congressman Wenstrup asked Fauci flat out at this hearing whether he's ever discussed viruses with US intelligence. >> But did you at any time talk to concerning viral research of any kind? >> Again, I say that at the time that there was concern about the fact that Al-Qaeda may have been using or potentially using bio-weapons, we had discussions with intelligence agencies about that >> Sure, but not as related to, say, COVID-19. >> Not to my knowledge about COVID. >> So, the man who built the network, ran the cover-up, and lied about it twice under oaths, which leaves the biggest question of all still open. What was this whole thing for? 40 labs in Ukraine alone. Gain of function work on pathogens that kill half the people they infect. A pandemic that killed millions and handed the world's would-be planners the exact emergency they'd been waiting for.
paragraphs.zh: 在听证会上，众议员温斯特普直截了当地问福奇，他是否曾就病毒问题与美国情报机构进行过讨论。>> 但你是否曾在任何时候就任何种类的病毒研究进行过交流？>> 我再次表示，当时人们担心基地组织可能正在使用或潜在使用生物武器，我们因此与情报机构就此进行了讨论。>> 当然，但这与新冠疫情无关。>> 据我所知，与新冠疫情无关。>> 因此，这位构建网络、主导掩盖并两次在宣誓下撒谎的人，留下了一个最大的问题仍未得到解答：这一切究竟是为了什么？仅在乌克兰就有40个实验室，对感染后致死率达一半的病原体进行功能增益研究。一场夺去数百万人生命的疫情，将世界既定的策划者们一直等待的紧急状态拱手相送。

paragraphs.en: That's not a string of accidents, that's a program. So, what was the program for? Start with Ukraine. The Russians were complaining about dangerous US biolabs on their border before the 2022 invasion.
paragraphs.zh: 那不是偶然事故的连锁反应，而是一个有计划的行动。那么，这个计划的目的何在？先从乌克兰说起。在2022年入侵之前，俄罗斯方面就抱怨其边境附近存在危险的美国生物实验室。

paragraphs.en: Think about it. COVID had already happened. Gain of function was clearly going on. Those complaints were waved away as propaganda, even though the Russian government named them as part of the reason for going to war.
paragraphs.zh: 仔细想想。新冠疫情已经发生。功能增益研究显然正在进行中。尽管俄罗斯政府将这些指控列为发动战争的原因之一，但这些投诉仍被当作宣传而轻描淡写地忽略。

paragraphs.en: Gabber's due 12th release confirms 40 US-funded labs in Ukraine, some doing gain of function work, many permitted for the most dangerous pathogens on Earth. One project involved a strain of avian influenza with a 50% human fatality rate. The Odessa lab's inventory included anthrax, tularemia, tuberculosis, MERS, SARS, Marburg, Ebola, Lassa, plague. Now, widen the lens.
paragraphs.zh: 加伯的第12份报告确认了乌克兰有40个由美国资助的实验室，其中一些正在进行增益功能研究，许多实验室被允许处理地球上最危险的病原体。其中一个项目涉及一种对人类致死率为50%的禽流感毒株。敖德萨实验室的库存包括炭疽、兔热病、结核病、中东呼吸综合征（MERS）、严重急性呼吸综合征（SARS）、马尔堡病毒、埃博拉病毒、拉沙热和鼠疫。现在，扩大视野。

paragraphs.en: COVID just didn't kill millions, it brought police state mandates, lockdowns, forced vaccines, and it was the pretext to rewrite election law across the United States, opening the door to the mail-in voting fraud which happened in the 2020 election. And over at Globish headquarters, the World Economic Forum, Klaus Schwab and Memphis Charles announced that the pandemic was the opportunity for a great reset of their entire system.
