articles.title: MAMDANI PANIC: His Bitter July 4th Attack on Musk Backfired — Then Trump and Vance Buried Him

paragraphs.en: Three speeches given by President Trump and Vice President Bance over the past 72 hours showed us how to confront our nation's enemies and rescue our young people. Gen Z and the millennials are the targets of the Democrats and Communists. Clearly, they have lived in the counterculture created under Presidents Obama and Biden and the teachers unions who control public education. Many of them are completely ignorant of our history and achievements.
paragraphs.zh: 在过去72小时里，特朗普总统和万斯副总统发表的三次演讲向我们展示了如何对抗我国的敌人并拯救我们的年轻人。Z世代和千禧一代是民主党和共产主义者的目标。显然，他们生活在奥巴马总统和拜登总统以及控制公立教育的教师工会所营造的反文化环境中。其中许多人对我们国家的历史和成就完全无知。

paragraphs.en: The deceased communist Antonio Grahamshy laid out the reason for this counterculture many years ago. He said that you can't make a communist revolution unless you get a country to hate its culture. I'm Bubba Boyd and I've tracked regime change operations of every variety for 50 years. What has been said by the president and vice president in the last 72 hours is the antidote to the poison of communism and socialism and the Democrat party's embrace of both.
paragraphs.zh: 已故的共产主义者安东尼奥·格雷厄姆什多年前就阐明了这种反文化现象的原因。他说，除非让一个国家憎恨自己的文化，否则就无法实现共产主义革命。我是巴比·博伊德，五十年来我一直在追踪各种形式的政权更迭行动。总统和副总统在过去72小时内所说的话，正是对共产主义、社会主义以及民主党对这两者的拥抱所释放出的毒药的解毒剂。

paragraphs.en: Today, I'm going to start by first showing you what our enemies said about our 250th anniversary in New York City by the new mayor Zoran Mandami. Then I'm going to recap the key parts of what the president of vice president said and did which left young people and others attending the Washington DC events with smiles on their faces and an overwhelming sense of purpose and joy. Now as July 4th 2026 approached, the intensity of attacks on the president's planned Freedom 250 celebration got louder and louder. Musicians pulled out because they didn't want to be identified with a successful Trump event.
paragraphs.zh: 今天，我首先向大家展示纽约市新市长佐兰·曼达米对我们250周年庆典的敌对言论。随后，我将回顾副总统在华盛顿特区活动中所说和所做的关键内容，这些言行让在场的年轻人和其他参与者面带微笑，并感受到强烈的使命感与喜悦。随着2026年7月4日的临近，针对总统计划举办的“自由250”庆典的攻击声浪愈发高涨。一些音乐人退出演出，因为他们不愿与特朗普取得成功的活动产生关联。

paragraphs.en: Blue states refused to participate in the great American state fair on the mall, claiming that the $100,000 being charged to fray security costs was beyond their state's means or a waste for a partisan effort celebrating Trump. Neo-Nazis calling themselves the Patriot Fund marched around DC in an obvious attempt to replay the SPLC's Charlottesville hoax from the first Trump administration. Then Chattam House, the foreign policy brain of the globalist establishment, chimed in. They said that Freedom 250 only displayed the fault lines which they would like to see tear us apart.
paragraphs.zh: 蓝州拒绝参加在华盛顿国家广场举行的大美利坚州博览会，声称收取的10万美元用于分摊安保费用超出了各州的承受能力，或是对一场庆祝特朗普的党派活动的浪费。自称“爱国者基金”的新纳粹分子在华盛顿特区游行，明显试图重演第一任特朗普时期南方贫困法律中心（SPLC）在夏洛茨维尔制造的骗局。随后，全球主义建制派的外交政策智囊——查塔姆研究所也加入了评论。他们表示，“自由250”活动仅展示了他们希望看到撕裂我们的分歧线。

paragraphs.en: Here's what they said. Today, as the US looks to celebrate its 250th anniversary, the most urgent fault line in US politics is between those who believe the current system of US democracy can provide rights, equity, and prosperity for all and those who do not. It is in fact the foundational fault line being relitigated for modern times. A long unthinkable question is being asked by Americans.
paragraphs.zh: 他们如是说。在美国即将迎来建国250周年之际，美国政治中最紧迫的分歧在于：一方坚信现行的美国民主体制能够为所有人带来权利、公平与繁荣，另一方则对此持怀疑态度。这实际上是当代背景下被重新争论的根本性分歧。美国人正在提出一个曾经难以想象的问题。

paragraphs.en: Is it necessary again to dissolve the political bands which connect them? They favorably cite Zoron Mandami and his gang of new communists in Congress as forebears of a new American civil war, one they're hoping for. But the Kappa was a speech given at New York City Hall by Mandami on July 3rd. He was obviously trying to counter what both Trump and Vance would say about the nature of America on July 4th.
paragraphs.zh: 是否真的有必要再次切断将他们联系在一起的种种政治纽带？他们援引佐伦·曼达米及其在新国会中的新共产党同伙，将其描绘成一场新的美国内战的先驱，而这正是他们所期盼的。然而，“卡帕”演讲是曼达米于7月3日在纽约市政厅发表的。他显然意在反驳特朗普和万斯在7月4日关于美国本质的言论。

paragraphs.en: Here's the heart of that speech. As we mark 250 years, what do we see? We see a city of contradictions within a nation of contradictions. We see the wealthiest country in the history of the world.
paragraphs.zh: 这是那场演讲的核心所在。在我们纪念二百五十周年之际，我们看到的是什么？我们看到的是一个充满矛盾的城市，身处一个充满矛盾的国家之中。我们看到的是世界上最富有的国家。

paragraphs.en: One where children go to sleep hungry while the world's first trillionaire hungers for more. We see monopolies that dominate every industry and oligarchs who buy elections. We see mass agents terrorizing our streets, eating food cooked by our undocumented neighbors before spiriting them away in unmarked vans. We see a nation whose immense wealth has been built by those with callous dirt streaked hands.
paragraphs.zh: 这是一个孩子们饿着肚子入睡，而世界上第一位万亿富翁却仍在渴求更多的世界。我们看到垄断企业主宰着每一个行业，寡头们购买选举。我们看到大批特工在我们的街道上制造恐怖，吃掉我们无证邻居做的食物，然后将他们悄无声息地用无标记的面包车带走。我们看到一个国家的巨大财富是由那些双手布满老茧和污垢的人所创造的。

paragraphs.en: Those who toil on factory floors and chisel into stone. And we see a nation that has allowed so much of that wealth to be held instead in the soft hands of a precious few. >> So the world's first trillionaire is Elon Musk. He invented the Starling communication system which revolutionized communications. He invented the reusable rockets which will f us to space and colleges on the moon and Mars.
paragraphs.zh: 那些在工厂车间辛勤劳作的人，那些在石头上精雕细琢的人。我们看到，一个国家竟让如此巨大的财富掌握在少数特权阶层手中。>> 因此，世界上第一位万亿富翁是埃隆·马斯克。他发明了星链通信系统，彻底改变了通信方式；他发明了可重复使用的火箭，将把人类送往太空，并在月球和火星上建立殖民地。

paragraphs.en: He engineered a remarkable electric car which outpaces anything Detroit has put on the street in that category. He bought Twitter and destroyed Biden's censorship regime. He is famous for sweeping on the factory floor and famous for designing factories in which production soarses. That man, Zoron says, has the soft hands which steal from workers.
paragraphs.zh: 他打造了一款令人瞩目的电动汽车，其性能远超底特律在该类别中推出的任何车型。他收购了推特，并推翻了拜登的审查制度。他因亲自在工厂车间劳作而闻名，也因设计出产量飙升的工厂而著称。然而，佐伦声称，这个人有着“柔软的双手”，却从工人手中窃取利益。

paragraphs.en: That's not all. Mandami makes a big point about delivering his dietry from George Washington's desk. But notice the picture. The desk is backwards.
paragraphs.zh: 这还不是全部。曼达米强调，他从乔治·华盛顿的书桌上递送他的膳食。但请注意图片，书桌是反着的。

paragraphs.en: Notice also the glum and fixed expressions of the newly naturalized citizens he has used as his props. The gray faces of communist victims from Soviet times looked much like this. The speech was widely panned thankfully. Our internet soldiers used the best super weapon against it which we have satire and ridicule.
paragraphs.zh: 此外，还要注意那些被他当作道具的新入籍公民们那阴郁而僵硬的神情。苏联时期共产主义受害者的灰暗面容与此极为相似。值得庆幸的是，这篇演讲遭到了广泛的批评。我们的“网络士兵”动用了对付它的最强超级武器——讽刺与嘲笑。

paragraphs.en: I particularly like this one. >> Hey guys, it's your mayor. Call me mom. Since we're communists and we don't have electricity for everyone except for ourselves, we would really appreciate it if you can go ahead and set your AC to a special mode called off mode. Also, it would be great if you could also stop eating because we don't have food for everyone.
paragraphs.zh: 我特别喜欢这一段。>> 大家好，我是你们的市长。叫我“妈妈”就行。既然我们是共产主义者，而我们只有我们自己能用上电，所以如果你们能把空调调到一种特别模式，也就是“关闭”模式，我们将不胜感激。另外，如果你们也能停止进食，那将再好不过，因为我们没有足够的食物供所有人食用。

paragraphs.en: So, if you're fat, please eat once every other day. If you're kind of fat, you can eat once a day. And if you're skinny, you're probably white, so you don't get to eat anything. If you have any pets, please bring them in to city hall so we can feed our Haitian immigrants.
paragraphs.zh: 所以，如果你比较胖，请每隔一天吃一顿饭；如果你有点胖，可以每天吃一顿；如果你很瘦，那你可能是白人，所以你不能吃任何东西。如果你有任何宠物，请把它们带到市政厅，以便我们喂养我们的海地移民。

paragraphs.en: Well, I'm off to enjoy Taylor Swift's wedding. So, later, comrades. But think about this attack on much genius and creativity. It gets to the heart of what President Trump and JD Vance talked about in the last 72 hours.
paragraphs.zh: 好吧，我要去享受泰勒·斯威夫特的婚礼了。再见，同志们。但请想想这场对大量天才和创造力的攻击。它触及了特朗普总统和JD万斯在过去72小时里讨论的核心问题。

paragraphs.en: Now, if what I'm telling you here tells you something you didn't consider about the current attacks on America or President Trump, subscribe to our free newsletter at Prometheianaction.com. We'll be giving you the true picture of this battle. That's what we do. On July 3rd, President Trump went to Mount Rushmore and gave a speech defining our unique culture.
paragraphs.zh: 现在，如果我在这里所讲述的内容让您对当前针对美国或特朗普总统的攻击有了此前未曾考虑到的新视角，请订阅我们在 Prometheianaction.com 提供的免费通讯。我们将为您呈现这场斗争的真实图景。这正是我们所做的工作。7月3日，特朗普总统前往拉什莫尔山，发表了一场阐述我们独特文化的演讲。

paragraphs.en: He didn't start by saying what we are not. You can't win the battle this way. You have to define what we are and fill out the picture with both the history and the dreams for our future which fit that definition. Pay attention as we play clips from that speech.
paragraphs.zh: 他没有从“我们不是什么”开始。用这种方式无法赢得这场战斗。你必须明确“我们是什么”，并通过历史与契合这一定义的未来愿景，完整地描绘出这幅图景。请留意我们接下来播放的那段演讲片段。

paragraphs.en: First, he talked about the presidents whose faces are engraved in Mount Rushmore. Here's what he said. These heroes exemplify what is timeless, enduring, and eternal about the American character. And in the end, it has always been that character, our distinct and unique identity.
paragraphs.zh: 首先，他谈到了刻在拉什莫尔山上的总统们。以下是他的发言：这些英雄体现了美国性格中那些永恒、持久且不朽的特质。归根结底，始终如一的正是这种性格，是我们独特而鲜明的身份认同。

paragraphs.en: It is a truly unique identity. And it'll never change. It's the ultimate source of our strength and the bull work of our freedom. on this anniversary. M we must remember, we have to remember, we can never forget that American liberty has not endured for 250 years merely because of words on paper.
paragraphs.zh: 这是一种真正独特的身份，而且永远不会改变。它是我们力量的终极源泉，也是我们自由的基石。在这个纪念日，我们必须铭记，永远不要忘记：美国的自由之所以历经250年而不衰，并非仅仅因为纸上的文字。

paragraphs.en: Liberty has prevailed here because of the culture and character of the people who declared it, defended it, and preserved it. >> Then he began his attack. But in recent years, there's been an undeniable attempt to change this exceptional character, to beat the American spirit out of us, alienate us from our history, and to make it impossible to even answer the question, what does it mean to be an American? As we march into our 250th year, incredible, beautiful year, it will be. We must never forget there is no American freedom without American culture. >> Now, he gives his definition of our culture. >> Our rights here given to us by the God who made us and those rights shall not be infringed.
paragraphs.zh: 自由之所以在此得以确立，是因为宣告、捍卫并守护它的那些人民所具备的文化与品格。>> 随后，他开始发起抨击。但近年来，人们不可否认地试图改变这种独特的品格，试图磨灭我们的美国精神，使我们与自身历史疏离，甚至让人无法再回答“何为美国人”这一问题。在我们迈向建国250周年的这一非凡而美好的一年之际，我们绝不能忘记：没有美国文化，就没有美国自由。>> 现在，他给出了他对美国文化的定义。>> 我们的权利由创造我们的上帝赋予，这些权利不可侵犯。

paragraphs.en: Americans believe in self-reliance. We look at success with envy not and I say that some people are envious and some people are not. We are not but with admiration and we earn it and we will always earn it and we will always respect it. We are an incredible good, kind, and generous people always ready to help a friend or a neighbor in need.
paragraphs.zh: 美国人崇尚自力更生。我们以钦佩而非嫉妒的眼光看待成功。并非所有人都怀有嫉妒之心，而我们则不然；我们满怀敬意，并通过自身努力赢得这份成就，也始终尊重这样的成就。我们是一个极其善良、仁慈且慷慨的民族，总是乐于帮助有需要的朋友或邻居。

paragraphs.en: No one has ever given more to charity, ended more hunger, cured more disease, or done more to uplift humanity than Americans. And no country ever will be able to match it. What is great can be made greater than ever before. And that's what's happening with America.
paragraphs.zh: 从未有任何人比美国人更慷慨地投身慈善事业，缓解更多饥饿，治愈更多疾病，或为提升人类福祉做出更大贡献。也从未有任何国家能够企及这一成就。伟大的事物，必将比以往任何时候都更加卓越——而美国正是如此。

paragraphs.en: Show us a mountain and we'll just climb it. Show us an ocean and we'll just cross it. Show us a problem and we will just solve it. show us a task the world calls impossible and Americans will get it done. >> Only then does he make the contrast. >> The American founding represents the best ideas and traditions in history by the best people like you. You can be loyal to Karl Marx or you can be loyal to America.
paragraphs.zh: 向我们展示一座山，我们就会去攀登；向我们展示一片海洋，我们就会去跨越；向我们展示一个问题，我们就会去解决；向我们展示一项被世界视为不可能完成的任务，美国人就会把它完成。>> 直到这时，他才进行对比。>> 美国的建国理念汇聚了历史上最伟大的人物所秉持的最佳思想和传统，就像你们这样的人。你可以忠于卡尔·马克思，也可以忠于美国。

paragraphs.en: You can be a communist or you can be a patriot. You cannot be both. As for those who pedal Marxist lies about our heritage, tell our children that we live on stolen land or that our heroes were oppressors. They're doing something much worse than slandering our past.
paragraphs.zh: 你可以做一个共产主义者，也可以做一个爱国者，但你不能两者兼而有之。至于那些散布马克思主义谎言、告诉我们我们的遗产是建立在窃取的土地之上，或者我们的英雄是压迫者的人，他们所做的远比诋毁我们的过去更为恶劣。

paragraphs.en: They are slandering and attacking our future. Not going to let that happen. They're trying to tear down the great American character to destroy the people who declared independence, who crossed the Delaware, who settled the West and conquered the skies. >> The rest of the speech is an elaboration of these ideas. On July 4th, JD Mast went to New York Harbor to attend a July 4th celebration presented by the city of New York and the US Navy.
paragraphs.zh: 他们正在诽谤并攻击我们的未来。我们不会让这种情况发生。他们试图摧毁伟大的美国精神，以抹黑那些宣告独立、横渡特拉华河、拓荒西部并征服天空的人们。>> 演讲的其余部分是对这些观点的进一步阐述。7月4日，JD·万斯前往纽约港，参加由纽约市和美国海军联合举办的独立日庆祝活动。

paragraphs.en: It was planned back in 2024 by the former mayor Eric Adams and the Navy and Mandami couldn't weasle his way out of it. You'll notice the same pattern in the speech by the vice president. First, he defines the American culture and American character. He starts by revisiting George Washington at New York Harbor at the start of the Revolutionary War.
paragraphs.zh: 这项计划早在2024年就已由时任市长埃里克·亚当斯与美国海军共同制定，曼达米无法从中脱身。你会注意到，副总统的演讲中也呈现出相同的模式。首先，他定义了美国文化和美国精神。他从回顾美国独立战争初期乔治·华盛顿在纽约港的情景开始。

paragraphs.en: Faced with a battle just ahead against the British Empire, the most powerful nation on earth, Washington chose to read to his men the Declaration of Independence, which had just been ratified in Philadelphia. It was new. They had to know what they were fighting for. But then, Bans points out something very important.
paragraphs.zh: 面对即将到来的与地球上最强大的国家——大英帝国的战斗，华盛顿选择向他的士兵们宣读刚刚在费城获得批准的《独立宣言》。这份文件是崭新的。他们必须清楚自己为何而战。然而，班斯指出了一件非常重要的事情。

paragraphs.en: These words were not new to the men Washington read them to. They were, as Jefferson put it, an expression of the American mind, an expression of the common idea at the time of what our culture was all about as understood by everyone. The vice president then elaborates real American stories of meeting new challenges without the credentials or experience today's standards would dictate. Here's Henry Kaiser, one of the examples he talked about.
paragraphs.zh: 华盛顿所宣读的这些话语，对在场的人来说并非陌生。正如杰斐逊所言，它们是“美国精神”的体现，反映了当时社会普遍认同的文化内涵，为众人所共知。随后，副总统进一步阐述了一些真实的美国故事，讲述人们如何在缺乏当今标准所要求的资质或经验的情况下，迎接新的挑战。以下是他所提及的一个例子：亨利·凯泽。

paragraphs.en: Now, he walked into a bank with no business, no workforce, and no history. Just a single proposed contract and what the banker would later describe as sheer audacity. He walked out with a $25,000 loan. And with his workers, he erected the Hoover Dam and hundreds of miles of highway across the West.
paragraphs.zh: 当时，他走进一家银行，没有业务、没有员工、也没有历史可言。他仅凭一份拟议中的合同，以及后来银行家形容为“纯粹胆量”的东西，成功获得了一笔2.5万美元的贷款。随后，他与工人们一起建造了胡佛水坝，并在美国西部修建了数百英里的高速公路。

paragraphs.en: Everywhere he went, he organized ordinary Americans to build extraordinary things. And everywhere he went, he believed their welfare and the welfare of the enterprise that he was building went hand in hand. Kaiser pioneered health coverage at his construction sites. That's why we know the name of Kaiser because he understood that men working in dangerous conditions, they deserve to see a doctor.
paragraphs.zh: 无论他走到哪里，他都组织普通美国人去创造非凡的成就。而且，无论他走到哪里，他都坚信员工的福祉与他所建设的企业的发展是相辅相成的。凯撒在其建筑工地率先推行健康保险。正因如此，我们才知晓凯撒之名，因为他明白，在危险环境中工作的工人理应得到医生的诊治。

paragraphs.en: He saw his workers as partners and he walked the walk long before it was fashionable to do so. Where some say that America's story is one of the dispossessed struggling against the powerful, Kaiser shows that America's greatness is built by cooperation between all of our citizens from every single walk of American life. Then Bass goes on to attack our enemies, saying, >> "They'll talk about America's sins with the anger and zeal of a brimstone preacher, but without any of the grace or forgiveness that must be present in the Christian faith. These people misunderstand the essence of America from Thomas Jefferson to Henry Kaiser.
paragraphs.zh: 他将自己的员工视为合作伙伴，远在他成为时尚之前，他就已身体力行。当有些人将美国的故事描绘为无权无势者对抗强权的抗争时，凯泽却向我们展示，美国的伟大建立在所有公民——来自美国社会各个阶层——之间的合作之上。随后，巴斯继续抨击我们的敌人，称：“他们会带着如布道者般的愤怒与狂热，谈论美国的罪过，却完全缺乏基督教信仰中应有的恩典与宽恕。这些人从托马斯·杰斐逊到亨利·凯泽，都未能理解美国的本质。”

paragraphs.en: They misunderstand that we all have moments of great power despite those very real imperfections. That there are victims and heroes who live inside of each of us. From the most ambitious leaders and builders to steel welders like my grandfather. People who had their own role to play in turning this dangerous wilderness into the proudest civilization in history.
paragraphs.zh: 他们误解了，我们每个人都有伟大的力量，尽管我们有着这些真实存在的缺点。在我们每个人心中，都有受害者和英雄的存在。从最有抱负的领导者和建设者，到我祖父那样的钢铁焊工，每个人都有自己在将这片危险的荒野转变为历史上最自豪的文明中所扮演的角色。

paragraphs.en: Now, 42,000 people attended the July 4th celebration on the National Mall in scorching heat before it was interrupted by dangerous weather. That count is probably from those who passed through magnetometers to get inside the event, although the media, as usual, tried to throw massive shade on it. Yesterday, I heard accounts of the event from people who stayed all the way through Trump's speech and record-breaking fireworks, including young people. They were tired but inspired and very very happy.
paragraphs.zh: 今天，在华盛顿国家广场上，约4.2万人顶着酷热参加了7月4日的庆祝活动，直到恶劣天气导致活动中断。这一数字可能仅统计了通过金属探测器安检进入活动现场的人数，而媒体一如既往地试图对此大加贬低。昨天，我听到了一些全程参与活动的民众的反馈，包括那些坚持听完特朗普演讲并观看创纪录烟花表演的人，其中不乏年轻人。他们虽然疲惫，但备受鼓舞，感到无比开心。

paragraphs.en: In his speech, the president focused on making the American flag more than just an abstract symbol. Although he didn't directly mention it, one of the newly minted congressional communists from New York says the flag is something she wipes her hands on. The president featured flags from critical events in our nation's history during the Revolution, the Civil War, other wars and conflicts. Each had been made or wielded by American heroes facing incredible odds.
paragraphs.zh: 在演讲中，总统着重强调要让美国国旗不仅仅是一个抽象的象征。尽管他没有直接提及，但一位来自纽约的新晋国会共产党议员曾表示，国旗是她用来擦手的布。总统在演讲中展示了美国历史上关键事件中的旗帜，包括独立战争、南北战争以及其他战争和冲突中的旗帜。每一面旗帜都由面对重重困难的美国英雄们制作或挥舞。

paragraphs.en: These flags now had living, breathing humans attached to them. American heroes. They met their challenges with imagination, daring, and incredible will and courage. Their deeds and actions make our nation's opponents appear in their true character as overprivileged children who know nothing of real history or humanity.
paragraphs.zh: 如今，这些旗帜上附着的是有血有肉、鲜活的人类——美国的英雄们。他们以想象力、胆识以及非凡的意志和勇气迎接挑战。他们的壮举与行动，使我们的国家的对手暴露出其真实面目：一群享有过度特权、对真实历史和人性一无所知的孩子。

paragraphs.en: As I said at the outset, our nation is currently facing a new challenge from those who want to destroy us. And between now and the midterms, we have a very big job to do. Reinjjecting the real history, culture, and optimism forcefully portrayed over the past 72 hours by the president and vice president of the United States. That's our job here at Prometheian Action.
paragraphs.zh: 正如我开篇所言，我国当前正面临来自那些企图颠覆我们的人的新挑战。从现在到中期选举期间，我们肩负着极为重要的使命：重新注入由美国总统和副总统在过去72小时中有力展现的真实历史、文化与乐观精神。这正是Prometheian Action在此所承担的职责。

paragraphs.en: If you haven't already, become a subscriber to our newsletter. You'll find more of this type of ammunition there. If you can, please contribute. It's obvious we need to grow and we can't do that without your generous help.
paragraphs.zh: 如果您尚未订阅我们的通讯，请立即订阅。您将在其中找到更多此类内容。如果您有能力，请慷慨捐助。显而易见，我们需要壮大，而这一目标的实现离不开您的鼎力支持。

paragraphs.en: This has been your Monday update. Thanks for listening. >> Please take a moment and subscribe to our free newsletter at prometheianaction.com.
paragraphs.zh: 以上就是您本周一的更新。感谢您的收听。>> 请花一点时间订阅我们在 prometheianaction.com 上的免费通讯。

articles.title: Trump Is Finishing 1776 — Why Mark Carney and the DSA Want Him Gone

paragraphs.en: As we head into the July 4th weekend, there are two very different definitions of revolution floating around. One of them involves what our founders did 250 years ago and what Donald Trump is doing right now. The other revolution is a cheap and poisonous imitation. Right now, it's a joint project of Canada's Prime Minister Mark Carney and the Democratic Socialists of America.
paragraphs.zh: 随着我们步入7月4日周末，目前流传着两种截然不同的“革命”定义。其中一种涉及250年前我们的建国先贤所做的事，以及唐纳德·特朗普目前正在做的事。另一种革命则是一种廉价且有害的仿制品。目前，它是加拿大总理马克·卡尼与美国民主社会主义者共同推动的项目。

paragraphs.en: The organization which just won three congressional seats in New York City and another one in Colorado yesterday. They want to destroy this country as we know it. Here's President Trump talking about the danger. >> Because it's not socialism, it's really communism. They use the word social democrat because it sounds so nice, but it's really communism we're talking about.
paragraphs.zh: 该组织昨日在纽约市赢得三个国会席位，并在科罗拉多州赢得一个席位。他们意图摧毁我们熟知的这个国家。以下是特朗普总统关于这一危险的讲话：>> 这并非社会主义，而是真正的共产主义。他们使用“社会民主党人”一词，因为听起来悦耳动听，但我们所谈论的实际上是共产主义。

paragraphs.en: I think it's the biggest threat to our nation there is, maybe since our founding. That includes World War II, World War II, September 11th. It includes the Pearl Harbor attack. I think this is the biggest threat to our nation. >> Is he exaggerating?
paragraphs.zh: 我认为这是我国面临的最大威胁，或许自建国以来前所未有。这包括第二次世界大战、9·11事件以及珍珠港事件。我认为这是我国面临的最大威胁。>> 他是否在夸大其词？

paragraphs.en: Greater than Pearl Harbor? World War I? World War II? In this video, I'll show you why the president is not exaggerating.
paragraphs.zh: 比珍珠港事件更严重？第一次世界大战？第二次世界大战？在这段视频中，我将说明为什么总统并没有夸大其词。

paragraphs.en: After all, other presidents have been killed over implementing the American system. I'm going to start by showing you what the president is doing which would make our founders proud. Then we'll talk about the fakery of Mark Carney. He's now the spokesman for the City of London's war against Trump.
paragraphs.zh: 毕竟，其他总统因推行美国体制而遇刺身亡。我将从展示总统所采取的、令我们的开国元勋们感到自豪的行动开始。随后，我们将探讨马克·卡尼的造假行径。他如今已成为伦敦金融城对抗特朗普的代言人。

paragraphs.en: He has radical allies to deploy, namely the Democratic Socialists of America. So, all revolutions are new beginnings. Here's Scott Bessent yesterday talking about our current economy. >> I think we are at the edge of an innovation wave that we are seeing here. We are reshoring manufacturing under President Trump's policies.
paragraphs.zh: 他拥有激进盟友可供调遣，即美国民主社会主义者。因此，所有革命都是崭新的开端。以下是斯科特·贝森特昨日就我们当前经济状况的发言：>> 我认为，我们正站在一波创新浪潮的边缘，这股浪潮正在这里显现。在特朗普总统的政策推动下，我们正将制造业回流。

paragraphs.en: The United States is the best place in the world to bring bring uh, build your factory, have tax certainty, regulatory certainty, which is something this administration has has really done, and energy certainty. And, you know, back to the gasoline prices, uh, dur- during the Iran conflict, the US economy powered through it. The economy's been very strong. We've been creating about a hundred seventy thousand dollar, I mean, a hundred seventy thousand jobs a month during the conflict, and, you know, I wouldn't be surprised, I haven't seen the number, if, uh, this month's job number is very strong. >> He's talking about an innovation way, new technologies, new inventions, in an economy already resilient enough to defy all the predictions of disaster from the Iran war.
paragraphs.zh: 美国是全球最佳的投资建厂之地，能够提供税收确定性、监管确定性——本届政府在这方面取得了显著成效——以及能源确定性。至于汽油价格，在伊朗冲突期间，美国经济依然强劲前行。整体经济表现十分稳健。在冲突期间，我们每月新增约17万个就业岗位。我尚未看到最新数据，但如果本月就业数据依然强劲，我也不会感到意外。>> 他所说的创新，指的是新技术和新发明，而美国经济本身已具备足够的韧性，足以抵御关于伊朗战争将引发灾难的所有预测。

paragraphs.en: All revolutions involve entirely new beginnings built on the best of the past. Tariffs, deregulation, capital expenditure tax cuts, Federal Reserve reform, all of it aimed at bringing our industrial base home in the first months of this administration. That is the foundation. But, it's supporting a map of new scientific and technological discoveries that will sustain this revolution for generations to come.
paragraphs.zh: 所有革命都以全新的开端为标志，这些开端建立在过去的最佳成果之上。关税政策、放松管制、资本支出减税、美联储改革，所有这些举措都旨在本届政府上任的最初几个月内，将我们的工业基础带回国内。这是我们的根基。但在此基础上，我们还绘制了一幅新的科学与技术发现蓝图，这些发现将支撑这场革命，惠及子孙后代。

paragraphs.en: And last week, the administration put it all together in a new initiative. President Trump's America First Resilience Strategy. It was released on June 23rd. It hasn't been covered at all.
paragraphs.zh: 上周，政府将所有内容整合为一项新倡议：特朗普总统的“美国优先韧性战略”。该战略于6月23日发布，但迄今未受到任何关注。

paragraphs.en: But, it's a revolution in how we think about national security and the future. Up until now, our approach has been reactive. A disaster happens, we respond, we recover. That's crisis management.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，这是一场关于我们如何思考国家安全与未来的革命。迄今为止，我们的应对方式一直是被动的：灾难发生，我们响应，然后恢复。这属于危机管理。

paragraphs.en: We've also lived through invented catastrophes that never happened. The population bomb, limits to growth, holes in the ozone, imminent climate catastrophe. Those were all designed by globalists to assert new methods of social control. The premise of Trump's new strategy, I'll quote, "No adversary or hazard should be able to hold America or our core interests at risk." The aim is to back that with technology and industrial programs in every state, making any crisis survivable before it happens.
paragraphs.zh: 我们也经历过那些从未发生的虚构灾难：人口炸弹、增长的极限、臭氧层空洞、迫在眉睫的气候灾难。这些都被全球主义者设计出来，以推行新的社会控制手段。特朗普新战略的前提是，正如他所言：“没有任何对手或危险能够使美国或其核心利益陷入风险。”其目标是通过在各州推行技术和工业项目，使任何危机在发生之前就能被有效应对和化解。

paragraphs.en: If you read the president's executive orders and his national security strategy, you'll see this plan built out brick by brick. All of it is on the White House website. Now, think about that alongside the wave of discoveries Alexander Hamilton mandated right after our revolution. The Revolutionary War left us flat broke.
paragraphs.zh: 如果你阅读总统的行政命令及其国家安全战略，你就会看到这个计划是一砖一瓦构建起来的。所有这些内容都在白宫网站上公布。现在，请将其与亚历山大·汉密尔顿在美国独立革命后不久强制推行的那一波发现联系起来。独立战争使我们一贫如洗。

paragraphs.en: We defeated the most powerful empire on earth through our ingenuity, but our nation was bankrupt. In shaping our recovery, Hamilton turned to the great lesson of that war. It's the human mind and the method of applying it that determines any economy and a nation's future. Here's what he wrote in his report on manufacturers.
paragraphs.zh: 我们凭借智慧击败了当时地球上最强大的帝国，但我们的国家却已破产。在塑造我们的复苏之路时，汉密尔顿从那场战争中汲取了深刻的教训。正是人类的思维及其运用方式，决定了一个国家的经济及其未来。以下是他在《制造业报告》中所写的内容。

paragraphs.en: "To cherish and stimulate the activity of the human mind by multiplying the objects of enterprise is not among the least considerable of the expedients by which the wealth of a nation may be promoted." Every new scene which is open to the busy nature of man to rouse and exert itself is the addition of a new energy to the general stock of effort. Hamilton's National Bank and credit policies made sure new discoveries got the support they needed to reach immediate use. That's how we built our way out of bankruptcy. Now, listen to Vice President Pence on President Trump's approach just yesterday. >> I do think fundamentally that Hamiltonian tradition is going to be what we see in the American right and will dominate American conservative economic thinking for the future, yeah, which is not laissez-faire.
paragraphs.zh: 通过拓展事业领域来珍视并激发人类心智的活力，是促进国家财富增长的重要手段之一，其重要性不容忽视。每一个为忙碌的人类天性所开启的新场景，都能使其振奋并发挥潜能，从而为整体的努力储备增添新的能量。汉密尔顿的国家银行和信用政策确保了新发现能够获得必要的支持，得以迅速投入实际应用。正是凭借这一路径，我们走出了破产的困境。现在，请聆听副总统彭斯昨日对特朗普总统做法的阐述。 >> 我坚信，汉密尔顿式的传统将成为美国右翼的核心，并将主导未来美国保守主义的经济思维，其核心并非自由放任。

paragraphs.en: It's actually much more about, you know, building the kind of tools, building the kind of infrastructure that allow human beings to flourish. >> Yeah. >> That allow national and native industries to flourish at the expense of a hyper-globalized economy. And I think those are the basic principles that are going to carry us into the future. But to me, it's it's fundamentally about the dignity of the human person. The economy is a tool to service the dig- dignity of the human person.
paragraphs.zh: 其实，这更多是关于构建那样的工具和基础设施，使人类能够蓬勃发展。>> 是的。>> 使国家和本土产业得以蓬勃发展，而不是以高度全球化的经济为代价。我认为这些基本原则将引领我们走向未来。但对我来说，这根本上关乎人的尊严。经济是服务于人的尊严的工具。

paragraphs.en: If a set of economic policies make it easier for a person to raise a family, to earn a living wage, to give back to their community, to maybe go to church on Sunday, or to actually spend some leisure time building the kind of life that matters, like that is the sort of thing that we want to be supportive of. >> If you want to learn more about this history and why it's been banished from our schools, you need to subscribe to our free newsletter at prometheanaction.com. You'll find a whole lot more of the background there. Now, let's look at Mark Carney's attempted counterrevolution. Carney has been on a speaking tour with policy what he says is a third way for middle countries against Donald Trump.
paragraphs.zh: 如果一套经济政策能够让人们更容易养育家庭、赚取维持生计的工资、回馈社区、或许还能在周日去教堂，或者真正腾出一些休闲时间，去构建那种有意义的生活，那么这类政策正是我们应当予以支持的。>> 如果你想了解更多关于这段历史，以及它为何被从我们的学校中抹去，请订阅我们在 prometheanaction.com 提供的免费通讯。在那里，你会发现更多背景信息。现在，让我们来看看马克·卡尼试图发起的反革命。卡尼正在进行一场演讲巡回活动，推广他所谓的“第三条道路”，旨在为中产阶级国家提供应对唐纳德·特朗普的政策方案。

paragraphs.en: He admits the old globalist financial order he thrived in is dead and something new has to be built. By middle countries, he means Europe, the UK, and whoever else they can sucker in. Listen to what Carney said in Ireland on June 13th. "Right now, many countries are concluding that they must develop greater strategic autonomy and that impulse is understandable.
paragraphs.zh: 他承认，曾经让他大发其财的旧全球主义金融秩序已经死亡，必须建立某种新秩序。所谓“中间国家”，他指的是欧洲、英国以及他们能够忽悠进来的其他任何国家。请听他在6月13日于爱尔兰发表的讲话：“眼下，许多国家正得出结论，认为它们必须发展更大的战略自主性，而这种冲动是可以理解的。”

paragraphs.en: When the rules no longer protect you, you must protect yourself. A country that cannot feed itself, fuel itself, or defend itself has few options." He went on, "Because governments and business long prioritized efficiency over resilience, we have all developed supply chains and trading relationships that create dependencies that can turn economic integration from an advantage into subordination." Doesn't Doesn't like Scott Bessent and Donald Trump? But then he adds these kickers. We're passionate about building strong economies as the means to just societies.
paragraphs.zh: 当规则不再保护你时，你必须自我保护。一个无法自给粮食、能源或自卫的国家，选择余地寥寥无几。”他接着说：“由于政府和商界长期以来将效率置于韧性之上，我们都形成了产生依赖关系的供应链和贸易关系，这使得经济一体化从一种优势转变为从属地位。”斯科特·贝森特和唐纳德·特朗普并不喜欢这样的观点。但随后他又补充了这些关键要点：我们热衷于建设强大的经济体，作为实现公正社会的途径。

paragraphs.en: Just societies where we take care of the vulnerable. Just societies where you can love who you want to love. Where everyone can be their entire selves and all have a chance to thrive. Climate change is no longer a warning.
paragraphs.zh: 一个公正的社会，我们关怀弱势群体；一个公正的社会，你可以爱你想爱的人；一个公正的社会，每个人都能成为完整的自己，并拥有繁荣发展的机会。气候变化已不再是一个警告。

paragraphs.en: It is here. Last month Ireland experienced its hottest May on record. Last year in Canada, forests larger than Ireland burned in the drought and the heat. So you build the strong economy through the climate agenda and service of multicultural bliss?
paragraphs.zh: 它就在眼前。上个月，爱尔兰经历了有记录以来最热的五月。去年，加拿大在干旱和高温中，烧毁的森林面积甚至超过了爱尔兰的国土面积。那么，你是要通过气候议程以及多元文化带来的幸福服务来构建强大的经济吗？

paragraphs.en: Canada is focused on building a dense web of connections and hard coalitions that work issue by issue with partners that share enough common ground to act together. He's talking here about the same coalition of the willing working to block President Trump's peace efforts in Ukraine. It's why we were the first non-European country to join the EU security action for Europe defense procurement program. That's the defense spending edge in the coalition of the willing is using to salvage their dying economies and keep the Ukraine war going.
paragraphs.zh: 加拿大致力于构建一张紧密的联系网络以及由志同道合者组成的坚固联盟，这些联盟在与拥有足够共同点、能够协同行动的伙伴之间，就各项议题逐一展开合作。他在此所指的，正是同一群“意愿联盟”成员，他们正联手阻挠特朗普总统在乌克兰问题上推动的和平努力。这也是为什么我们成为首个加入欧盟“欧洲防务采购计划”安全行动的非欧洲国家。这一“意愿联盟”正利用其在防务开支方面的优势，来挽救其日益衰败的经济，并继续维持乌克兰战争的持续进行。

paragraphs.en: It's why we're championing efforts to build a bridge between the Trans-Pacific Partnership and the European Union. Something that would create a trading block of 1.5 billion people. Even Hillary Clinton knew the Trans-Pacific Partnership was a bad deal for working-class wages here. This all makes sense when you know Carney's background.
paragraphs.zh: 这正是我们大力推动在《跨太平洋伙伴关系协定》与欧盟之间搭建桥梁的原因。此举将形成一个涵盖15亿人口的贸易集团。就连希拉里·克林顿也承认，《跨太平洋伙伴关系协定》对美国工人阶级的工资水平而言是一笔糟糕的交易。在了解卡尼的背景之后，这一切便顺理成章了。

paragraphs.en: He headed both the Central Bank of Canada and the Bank of England. In 2019 at Jackson Hole, he called for digital currencies and all finance to be handled by banking experts rather than political relics like the US Congress. He He headed the net zero Climate Alliance, aiming to cut off credit worldwide to fossil fuel producers. That was before he ran for Canada's premiership at the urging and with the policies of his close friend, the Fabian socialist Tony Blair.
paragraphs.zh: 他曾先后担任加拿大央行行长和英格兰银行行长。2019年，在杰克逊霍尔全球央行年会上，他呼吁数字货币及所有金融事务应由银行专家而非像美国国会这样的政治遗存来管理。他还领导了“净零气候联盟”，旨在切断对化石燃料生产商的全球信贷。此后，在好友、费边社会主义者托尼·布莱尔的敦促下，并采纳其政策建议，他参选加拿大总理。

paragraphs.en: Carney has obviously been chosen to lead the UK and Europe against Trump's America First agenda, operating with all the deviousness of a British Fabian socialist. And a central banker attempting a revolution or coup needs foot soldiers on the ground. That's where the DSA comes in. Listen carefully as I outline the DSA's real program and its roots in the British Labour Party and British strategic operations against the empire's chosen enemies throughout history.
paragraphs.zh: 显然，卡尼已被选定为英国和欧洲对抗特朗普“美国优先”议程的领军人物，其行事风格带有英国费边社会主义者特有的狡黠与权谋。一位试图发动革命或政变的中央银行家，需要在基层拥有大量支持者。这正是美国民主社会主义者协会（DSA）发挥作用的地方。请仔细聆听，我将阐述DSA的真实纲领及其在英国工党中的根源，以及其在历史上针对帝国选定敌人所开展的战略行动。

paragraphs.en: The DSA's demands for free housing, health care, child care, education, and transportation sound like Europe's social democracies. That's because the DSA came from the Second Socialist International, the same organization that birthed the British Labour Party. They call these demands the minimum program. These demands, and I'm quoting, do not contend an immediate break with capitalism, but they heighten the contradictions of capitalism and represent a step toward its dissolution.
paragraphs.zh: 美国民主社会主义者（DSA）提出的免费住房、医疗保健、托儿服务、教育和交通等要求，听起来很像欧洲的社会民主主义模式。这是因为DSA源自第二国际，而英国工党也正是在该组织中诞生的。他们将这些要求称为“最低纲领”。正如他们所言，这些要求并不主张立即与资本主义决裂，但它们会加剧资本主义的矛盾，并代表迈向其消亡的一步。

paragraphs.en: In DSA's own theoretical journal, Cosmonaut Magazine, they say the minimum program is, quote, a road map for the proletariat to seize state power entirely in a revolutionary break. The 2025-2026 DSA platform, Workers Deserve More, calls explicitly for public ownership over major transportation and energy infrastructure and natural resources under its Green New Deal plan. Its preamble states the maximum goal, a socialist society where the largest corporations are put under public ownership and democratic control, where workers around the world join together in common struggle to construct socialism worldwide. The platform also calls for overturning the Constitution.
paragraphs.zh: 在民主社会主义者（DSA）自己的理论期刊《宇航员杂志》（Cosmonaut Magazine）中，他们指出，最低纲领是“无产阶级通过革命性断裂全面夺取国家权力的路线图”。DSA 2025-2026 年平台《工人理应获得更多》（Workers Deserve More）在其“绿色新政”计划中，明确呼吁对主要交通和能源基础设施以及自然资源实行公有化。该平台的前言阐明了其最高目标：建立一个社会主义社会，其中最大的企业被置于公有化和民主控制之下，全世界的工人联合起来，为在全球范围内建设社会主义而共同斗争。该平台还呼吁推翻现行宪法。

paragraphs.en: They want to abolish the Senate and make the Supreme Court and the president subservient to Congress. The stage for this stance has been set by strict adherence to Antonio Gramsci's communist command. To win the revolution, you must first get the targeted country to hate their own culture. It destroys any will to fight.
paragraphs.zh: 他们希望废除参议院，并使最高法院和总统屈从于国会。这种立场的基础在于严格遵循安东尼奥·葛兰西的共产主义指令。为了赢得革命，你必须首先让目标国家憎恨自己的文化。这会摧毁任何抵抗的意志。

paragraphs.en: That cultural war has been ongoing for some time now and has been supported by every major institution. Just think of the New York Times 1619 project portraying our founders as a bunch of land-stealing racists. Starting with Lord Palmerston, British intelligence has always used killer formations to destabilize its geopolitical enemies. The author, Richard Poe, documents how after World War I, the British engineered the Russian Revolution using Karl Marx's hatred of Russia.
paragraphs.zh: 这场文化战争已经持续了相当长的一段时间，并且得到了各大主要机构的支持。不妨想想《纽约时报》的“1619项目”，它将我们的建国先贤描绘成一群偷窃土地的种族主义者。从帕默斯顿勋爵开始，英国情报机构就一直利用杀手组织来破坏其地缘政治对手的稳定。作者理查德·波记录了在一战之后，英国如何利用卡尔·马克思对俄罗斯的仇恨来策划俄国革命。

paragraphs.en: The goal was to permanently destabilize both Russia and Germany. The Trump administration openly embraces Hamilton's American system to revive the United States, but the British have killed our presidents over that over time, from Lincoln to McKinley to Kennedy to their direct threats against President Trump. The American system, attacked by both finance capitalism and Karl Marx, is the only legitimate third way. And right now, we're in a war between the American system and the modern British financial system.
paragraphs.zh: 其目标是永久性地使俄罗斯和德国陷入不稳定。特朗普政府公开拥抱汉密尔顿式的美国体系，以重振美国，但英国人长期以来为此杀害了我们的总统，从林肯到麦金莱，再到肯尼迪，甚至对特朗普总统发出直接威胁。这一由金融资本主义和卡尔·马克思共同攻击的“美国体系”，是唯一合法的“第三条道路”。而当下，我们正处在“美国体系”与现代英国金融体系之间的战争之中。

paragraphs.en: Like all wars, some battles are won, some have been lost. But it will ultimately be decided by people who step outside the mental change our enemy puts on us through a corrupt media, a still corrupt Washington, and the psyops against the coalition that won in November. All launched by a desperate, dying globalist empire. Now they've tried to censor the American system out of our history book for decades, but we've discovered there's something like an American reflex still in our souls that answers great challenges and things people call impossible.
paragraphs.zh: 和所有战争一样，有些战役取得了胜利，有些则遭遇了失败。但最终的决定权将掌握在那些能够挣脱敌人通过腐败媒体、依然腐败的华盛顿以及对11月获胜的联盟所发动的心理战而强加于我们的心灵枷锁之外的人们手中。这一切都是由一个垂死挣扎的全球主义帝国所发起的。多年来，他们试图将美国体系从我们的历史书中抹去，但我们发现，我们的灵魂深处仍有一种美国式的本能反应，它回应着巨大的挑战，以及人们所称的“不可能之事”。

paragraphs.en: That's what has to be mobilized now for a massive win in the midterms. That's our mission at Promethean Action. If you haven't already, subscribe to our newsletter for yourself and others and contribute what you can. This has been your Wednesday update going into our 250th anniversary.
paragraphs.zh: 这就是如今必须动员起来，以在中期选举中取得重大胜利的力量。这正是我们在普罗米修斯行动中的使命。如果您尚未订阅我们的通讯，请为您自己和他人订阅，并尽您所能做出贡献。这是我们在迎接250周年庆典之际的周三更新。

paragraphs.en: Thanks so much for listening. >> Please take a moment and subscribe to our free newsletter at prometheanaction.com.
paragraphs.zh: 非常感谢你的聆听。>> 请花一点时间订阅我们在 prometheanaction.com 上的免费通讯。

articles.title: Nixon's Revenge: Trump Just Ended the Kissinger Doctrine — And Britain Knows It

paragraphs.en: Speaking at the Nixon Library last week, Vice President J.D. Vance dropped a bombshell. >> And by the way, if you look at the story of how the deep state took down Richard Nixon, it's not all that different from what the same groups of people, the same institutions tried to do to Donald Trump in the first Trump administration. There is a a parallel. >> And a few minutes later in that interview, the Vice President gave us a hint as to why both presidents have been targeted. He was reflecting on the epitaph on Nixon's memorial, and he quoted it. >> honor history can bestow is to call someone a peacemaker. >> If you peel back the curtain on the Vice President's comments about the deep state forces who went after both presidents and their fight for peace, you'll discover the hidden truth about the British hand in a century of war and Henry Kissinger's role in perpetuating many of them. I'm Susan Kokinda.
paragraphs.zh: 上周在尼克松图书馆发表讲话时，副总统J.D.万斯抛出了一枚重磅炸弹。>> 顺便说一句，如果你审视“深层政府”如何扳倒理查德·尼克松的故事，你会发现它与同一批人、同一批机构在第一任特朗普政府期间试图对唐纳德·特朗普做的事情并无太大区别。两者之间存在明显的平行关系。>> 在随后的几分钟里，这位副总统在采访中还透露了两位总统为何遭到针对的线索。他回顾尼克松纪念碑上的铭文，并引用道：>> 荣誉所能赋予的最高称号，莫过于称某人为“和平缔造者”。>> 如果你拨开副总统关于“深层政府”力量对两位总统及其追求和平之举进行打压的言论帷幕，你将发现隐藏在背后、关于英国在百年战争中的角色以及亨利·基辛格在其中助长诸多冲突的真相。我是苏珊·科金达。

paragraphs.en: And not only did I watch Watergate unfold from a front-row seat in Washington, I fought it, exposing it as a CIA coup against a sitting president. Here's what I'm going to cover today. How J.D. Vance's comments open up a window on the battle between American foreign policy and Henry Kissinger's British-scripted policies. How Lebanon became one of the first victims of Kissinger's policies and how this administration is finally reversing that.
paragraphs.zh: 我不仅坐在华盛顿的“前排座位”亲眼目睹了水门事件的发酵过程，还亲自参与了对它的揭露，将其定性为中央情报局针对一位在任总统的政变。今天，我将重点探讨以下内容：J.D.万斯的言论如何为我们提供了一扇窗口，让我们得以审视美国外交政策与亨利·基辛格所推行的、源自英国剧本的政策之间的博弈；黎巴嫩如何成为基辛格政策的最早受害者之一；以及本届政府如何最终扭转了这一局面。

paragraphs.en: And how a weapons test in New Mexico points to the end of Kissinger's strategic doctrine. So, let's go back to what else the Vice President said at the Nixon Library. In addition to coming right out and saying that the deep state had targeted both Nixon and Trump. >> I'm actually fascinated by Nixon as a character in history. I think that his historical legacy is enjoying a bit of a renaissance, but I think deservedly so.
paragraphs.zh: 以及新墨西哥州的一次武器试验如何指向基辛格战略学说的终结。那么，让我们回到副总统在尼克松图书馆发表的其他言论。除了直言不讳地表示“深层政府”将尼克松和特朗普都列为目标之外，他还表示：>> 我对尼克松作为历史人物这一点深感着迷。我认为，他的历史遗产正迎来一场复兴，而且这种复兴是当之无愧的。

paragraphs.en: He was actually like a political genius. And I'm just fascinated by the guy's historical figures because, you know, one of the other lessons of Richard Nixon is it's not just that he got out of Vietnam, but that he got out of Vietnam from a position of strength. >> Did you catch that? Out of Vietnam. Because Richard Nixon was the second post-war Republican president who had the same job.
paragraphs.zh: 他实际上堪称一位政治天才。我对这位历史人物始终着迷，因为从理查德·尼克松身上我们可以汲取的另一条教训是：他不仅让美国撤出了越南，而且是以强势地位实现撤军的。>> 你注意到这一点了吗？从越南撤军。因为理查德·尼克松是战后第二位担任同一职务的共和党总统。

paragraphs.en: Get the United States out of one of Britain's perpetual wars. Here's the pattern nobody connects for you. Before Nixon ended Vietnam, Dwight Eisenhower ended Korea. And Eisenhower did something even bigger.
paragraphs.zh: 让美国摆脱英国的一场又一场无休止的战争。这里有一个没人会为你点明的模式。在尼克松结束越南战争之前，艾森豪威尔已经结束了朝鲜战争。而艾森豪威尔还做了一件更为重大的事。

paragraphs.en: In 1956, when Britain, France, and Israel moved to seize the Suez Canal, the American president slapped it down and forced them out. Let that sink in. An American president telling London they didn't have free reign in the Middle East. Then a decade later, Eisenhower did something even more stunning.
paragraphs.zh: 1956年，当英国、法国和以色列企图夺取苏伊士运河时，美国总统予以坚决反对，并迫使它们撤出。请仔细体会这一事实：一位美国总统明确告诉伦敦，美国在中东地区并非任由其摆布。然而，十年后，艾森豪威尔总统又做了一件更为惊人的事。

paragraphs.en: He outlined an American approach to the Middle East. In 1967, near the end of his life, he wrote a piece in Reader's Digest along with the former head of the Atomic Energy Commission. They proposed bringing the Atoms for Peace vision to the Middle East with nuclear power desalination, which would turn the desert into farmland and provide water and power for Arabs and Israelis alike. Shared peace and prosperity, not managed hatred.
paragraphs.zh: 他勾勒出一套美国处理中东问题的方案。1967年，在他生命即将走到尽头之际，他与前美国原子能委员会主席在《读者文摘》上发表了一篇文章，提出将“和平利用原子能”的理念引入中东，通过核能海水淡化技术，将沙漠变为农田，为阿拉伯人和以色列人同时提供水和电力。他们倡导的是共享的和平与繁荣，而非被管理的仇恨。

paragraphs.en: Now, Richard Nixon's first Secretary of State, William Rogers, came out of the Eisenhower administration. He had been Ike's Attorney General. Take note. Rogers was Secretary of State.
paragraphs.zh: 现在，理查德·尼克松的第一任国务卿威廉·罗杰斯来自艾森豪威尔政府。他曾担任艾森豪威尔的司法部长。请注意，罗杰斯当时是国务卿。

paragraphs.en: Henry Kissinger was National Security Advisor in that first term. In the wake of the 1967 Six-Day War between Israel and Egypt, Rogers proposed a comprehensive plan for real Arab-Israeli peace. The purpose of Rogers' plan was to end the conflict, not perpetually manage it. And that's the same tradition Donald Trump has revived decades later with the Abraham Accords.
paragraphs.zh: 在第一任期内，亨利·基辛格担任国家安全顾问。在1967年以色列与埃及之间的六日战争之后，罗杰斯提出了一项旨在实现阿拉伯国家与以色列全面和平的计划。罗杰斯计划的目的并非永久性地管理冲突，而是结束冲突。几十年后，唐纳德·特朗普通过《亚伯拉罕协议》复兴了这一传统。

paragraphs.en: Which, like Eisenhower's original proposal, focuses on the economic development of sovereign nations and their shared prosperity. And it's the same tradition that Franklin Roosevelt put forward at Casablanca. Challenging Churchill about the British 18th century methods which take more out of a country than they put back in. Roosevelt wanted the colonies to be freed from British and French colonialization.
paragraphs.zh: 这与艾森豪威尔最初的提案一样，强调主权国家的经济发展和共同繁荣。这一理念也源自富兰克林·罗斯福在卡萨布兰卡会议期间所提出的主张。他挑战丘吉尔，批评英国自18世纪以来那种从殖民地榨取多于回馈的做法。罗斯福主张让殖民地摆脱英国和法国的殖民统治。

paragraphs.en: And develop them with American economic methods. But Roosevelt's challenge to Churchill died with him. And the Eisenhower-Rogers tradition ran smack-dab into Henry Kissinger. Britain's man on the inside.
paragraphs.zh: 并运用美国的经济学方法加以发展。然而，罗斯福对丘吉尔提出的挑战，随着他的逝世而烟消云散。艾森豪威尔—罗杰斯一脉的传统，径直与亨利·基辛格相遇。而基辛格，正是英国安插在美国内部的关键人物。

paragraphs.en: Now, before I get to Kissinger's role in the Nixon administration and how Donald [clears throat] Trump is reversing the damage, I want to encourage you to subscribe to Promethean Actions free newsletter. We'll give you the real background you need to cut through the noise and understand what the president is actually doing. The link is in the description. So, who was Henry Kissinger and why do I call him Britain's man on the inside?
paragraphs.zh: 现在，在我深入探讨基辛格在尼克松政府中的角色，以及唐纳德[清嗓子]特朗普如何扭转其造成的损害之前，我想鼓励大家订阅“普罗米修斯行动”（Promethean Actions）的免费通讯。我们将为您提供必要的真实背景，帮助您拨开迷雾，真正理解总统正在采取的行动。链接已在描述中提供。那么，亨利·基辛格究竟是谁？我为何称他为“英国安插在美国内部的代理人”？

paragraphs.en: Well, for starters, he admitted it. In 1982, he gave a speech to Britain's premier think tank Chatham House where he said, "In my White House incarnation then, I kept the British Foreign Office better informed and more closely engaged than I did the American State Department." By the way, William Rogers was running the State Department. But, it's deeper than who he answered to. Kissinger's entire foreign policy was based on the British balance of power doctrine.
paragraphs.zh: 首先，他本人对此予以承认。1982年，他在英国顶级智库查塔姆研究所发表演讲时说道：“在我担任白宫要职期间，我对英国外交部的信息通报更为及时，互动也更为紧密，远胜于对美国国务院。”顺便一提，当时美国国务院由威廉·罗杰斯领导。但问题远不止于他向谁负责。基辛格的外交政策整体上是建立在英国的权力平衡学说之上的。

paragraphs.en: The doctrine of Metternich, the Congress of Vienna, that later got repackaged as the geopolitics of the British Round Table. Kissinger never wanted the kind of peace Eisenhower and Rogers were after. His doctrine has one rule. You never actually solve a conflict.
paragraphs.zh: 梅特涅的学说，维也纳会议，后来被重新包装为英国圆桌会议的地缘政治。基辛格从未追求艾森豪威尔和罗杰斯所追求的那种和平。他的学说只有一条规则：你永远不会真正解决冲突。

paragraphs.en: You manage it. You pit nations or various forces against each other so that no one is ever strong enough or peaceful enough to break free. So, when William Rogers pushed his comprehensive peace plan for the Middle East, Kissinger pushed the other way. Nixon was increasingly cornered by Watergate and he gave in to Kissinger.
paragraphs.zh: 你掌控局势，挑拨各国或各方势力相互对抗，使任何一方都不足以强大到或稳定到能够挣脱束缚。因此，当威廉·罗杰斯提出其中东全面和平计划时，基辛格却持反对立场。尼克松因水门事件而日益陷入困境，最终向基辛格妥协。

paragraphs.en: By September 1973, Rogers was out. Kissinger replaced him as Secretary of State and not long after the Yom Kippur War broke out, the peace plan died, and the wars came back. And Lebanon was thrown into the meat grinder. Watch how the doctrine works.
paragraphs.zh: 到1973年9月，罗杰斯已经离职。基辛格接替了他担任国务卿，不久之后，赎罪日战争爆发，和平计划随之夭折，战火重燃，黎巴嫩也被卷入这场残酷的冲突漩涡。请看这一政策是如何运作的。

paragraphs.en: In the mid-1970s, Kissinger orchestrated a proposal that drew a red line across Lebanon, luring Syria in. By 1974, Kissinger's shuttle diplomacy throughout the Middle East was in full swing, and by 1975, Lebanon was in a civil war. And it stayed that way. A permanently managed conflict zone, which is finally being addressed by Donald Trump's administration.
paragraphs.zh: 20世纪70年代中期，基辛格策划了一项提议，在黎巴嫩划下红线，诱使叙利亚卷入。到1974年，基辛格在整个中东地区穿梭外交的活动正如火如荼地进行；而到了1975年，黎巴嫩已陷入内战，并且此后长期维持着这种状态。这片被永久管控的冲突地带，如今正由唐纳德·特朗普政府着手处理。

paragraphs.en: Because on April 14th, 2026, just a little over 2 months ago, in the midst of US operations against Iran, the Lebanese and Israeli ambassadors to the United States met face-to-face with Secretary Rubio for the first time in 43 years. And then last week, on June 26, Israel, Lebanon, and the United States signed a trilateral framework to end the conflict Henry Kissinger started. Listen to how Marco Rubio framed it. >> We are happy to announce the a framework agreement between the sovereign government of of Lebanon and and of course the government of Israel with a mediation and support of the United States of America that begins to put in place a framework for lasting peace and security. The people of Lebanon have suffered tremendously now for decades as a result of outside interference in their affairs.
paragraphs.zh: 因为就在2026年4月14日，也就是两个多月前，在美国对伊朗开展军事行动期间，黎巴嫩和以色列驻美国大使在43年来首次与美国国务卿鲁比奥面对面会晤。随后在上周的6月26日，以色列、黎巴嫩和美国签署了一项三方框架，以结束由亨利·基辛格开启的冲突。听听马尔科·鲁比奥是如何表述这一事件的。>> 我们很高兴宣布，在黎巴嫩主权政府与以色列政府之间，在美国的调解和支持下，达成了一项框架协定，该协定开始为持久和平与安全建立框架。数十年来，黎巴嫩人民因外部势力干涉其内政而遭受了巨大苦难。

paragraphs.en: What they deserve to have is what they once had and of which there is recent history of and that is a prosperous and peaceful country. A diverse country where people of different backgrounds were able to live and go coexist side by side and in many ways was the envy of the region and of the world. >> Secretary Roubini though did say it will take time. But we are in a different world than the world Henry Kissinger bequeathed to the nations of the Middle East. Just listen for a moment as an example to the closing words of the Israeli ambassador.
paragraphs.zh: 他们理应拥有的，是曾经拥有、且近期历史所见证的那样——一个繁荣而和平的国家。那是一个多元包容的国家，不同背景的人们能够和谐共处，并在许多方面成为该地区乃至世界的典范。鲁比尼国务卿虽然表示，实现这一目标需要时间，但我们所处的世界，与亨利·基辛格留给中东各国的那个世界已大不相同。不妨听一听以色列大使在结尾处的讲话，以此为例。

paragraphs.en: God bless Israel. May God bless Lebanon. May God bless the United States of America and all those who pursue peace. We are looking at the potential for peace and sovereignty, not balance of power for ever conflict.
paragraphs.zh: 愿上帝保佑以色列。愿上帝保佑黎巴嫩。愿上帝保佑美利坚合众国以及所有追求和平的人们。我们着眼于和平与主权的前景，而非为每一次冲突寻求权力平衡。

paragraphs.en: But settling one region's wars is not the whole revolution because Kissinger ran the same doctrine on a global scale and it held the entire human race hostage. And last week out in the desert that one cracked, too. On June 23rd out at the White Sands Testing Grounds in New Mexico, Secretary of War Pete Hegseth stood and watched American directed energy weapons burn drones out of the sky and demonstrate the proof of principle meant to take down far harder threats. The United States is now developing technologies that were proposed over four decades ago and which were throttled by none other than Henry Kissinger.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，平息一个地区的战争并非革命的全部，因为基辛格将同一套学说在全球范围内推行，并将整个人类种族扣为人质。而上周，在沙漠中，这一枷锁也出现了裂痕。6月23日，在新墨西哥州的白沙试验场，美国战争部长皮特·海格塞斯站立观看美国定向能武器将无人机击落，并展示了旨在应对更为严峻威胁的原理性验证。如今，美国正在研发的技术正是四十多年前就被提出、却由亨利·基辛格本人亲手扼杀的那些技术。

paragraphs.en: Because in 1972, Henry Kissinger locked in the anti-ballistic missile treaty. A treaty whose entire purpose was to limit missile defense. That was the ultimate implementation of the doctrine of mutual and assured destruction or mad. Ronald Reagan tried to end it when he announced the Strategic Defense Initiative on March 23rd, 1983.
paragraphs.zh: 因为1972年，亨利·基辛格促成了《反导条约》的签署。该条约的全部目的就在于限制导弹防御系统。这正是“相互确保摧毁”（Mutual Assured Destruction，简称MAD）战略的最终体现。1983年3月23日，罗纳德·里根宣布启动“战略防御倡议”，试图终结这一局面。

paragraphs.en: He wanted to render nuclear weapons impotent and obsolete, his quote. I testified in congressional hearings supporting the SDI and describing how it would be a science driver for the economy. But Kissinger's imperial overlords couldn't allow it. It was mocked as Star Wars and it was killed by the budget process.
paragraphs.zh: 他希望使核武器失去效力并变得过时，这是他的原话。我在国会听证会上作证，支持战略防御倡议（SDI），并阐述它将如何成为推动经济发展的科技引擎。然而，基辛格背后的帝国权贵们却不容许此事发生。该计划被讥讽为“星球大战”，并最终在预算程序中被扼杀。

paragraphs.en: But, look at what happened as soon as Donald Trump became president. In January 2025, he signed Executive Order 14186, which calls for an Iron Dome, now called the Golden Dome, for our country. And it says, the United States will deter and defend its citizens and critical infrastructure against any foreign aerial attack on the homeland. Did you get that?
paragraphs.zh: 但请看唐纳德·特朗普就任总统后所发生的一切。2025年1月，他签署了第14186号行政令，要求为我国建立“铁穹”系统，现称为“金穹”。该行政令明确指出，美国将威慑并防御其公民及关键基础设施，免受任何针对本土的外国空中攻击。你听清楚了吗？

paragraphs.en: Defend, not retaliate. Defend. And the order names Ronald Reagan and his efforts. And it ends Kissinger's policies of staying defenseless.
paragraphs.zh: 防御，而非报复。防御。这一命令以罗纳德·里根及其努力命名，并终结了基辛格所推行的防御无力的政策。

paragraphs.en: Assured destruction is now answered with assured defense. That's the doctrine. And White Sands is the proof that it's real. But there's another aspect to it.
paragraphs.zh: “确保相互摧毁”如今已被“确保防御”所回应。这就是该战略原则。而白沙试验场正是其真实存在的证明。但除此之外，还有另一个层面。

paragraphs.en: Defense blog put it this way. A laser weapon with a cost per shot measured in dollars of electricity and a magazine limited only by the power supply feeding it inverts the economic equation entirely. This is dramatic when it comes to the drone threat. Rather than knocking out drones with enormously expensive conventional weapons, directed energy weapons flip the equation.
paragraphs.zh: 防务博客曾这样表述：激光武器的单次发射成本仅以几美元的电力费用计算，而其弹药容量仅受限于为其供电的电源，这彻底颠覆了传统的经济逻辑。在应对无人机威胁时，这一特点尤为显著。定向能武器不再依赖价格高昂的传统武器来摧毁无人机，而是从根本上扭转了成本效益的格局。

paragraphs.en: The thing that made defense too expensive is gone by the discovery of new physical principles. And the spending goes beyond drone defense, it does include missile defense. The Army and Navy are spending $676 million over five years under the joint laser weapon system to develop a laser capability for defeating incoming cruise missiles. And the Pentagon told Congress in May of this year that a commitment was made to the president to include directed energy in a golden dome demonstration by summer 2028.
paragraphs.zh: 使防御成本过高的因素，因新物理原理的发现而得以消除。相关支出不仅涵盖无人机防御，还包括导弹防御。美国陆军和海军在“联合激光武器系统”项目下，将在五年内投入6.76亿美元，以开发用于拦截来袭巡航导弹的激光能力。此外，五角大楼在今年5月向国会表示，已承诺总统，将在2028年夏季前完成“金穹顶”演示中纳入定向能武器。

paragraphs.en: But here's the whole ballgame. A shield is only as real as the nation that can build it. And that's why Hegseth is sending what he is calling a demand signal to revive America's defense industrial base and drag these weapons out of what is called the valley of death and into production. And right there is the heart of everything.
paragraphs.zh: 但问题的核心在于：盾牌只有在能够建造它的国家手中才具有现实意义。正因如此，赫格塞斯正在发出他所称的“需求信号”，以重振美国的国防工业基础，并将这些武器从所谓的“死亡之谷”带入量产阶段。而这正是所有问题的关键所在。

paragraphs.en: The empire's doctrine is looting, backwardness, and perpetual war. The American answer is to build, to manufacture, to invent, to develop. That is the American system. It's what Eisenhower carried, it's what William Rogers fought for, it's what Reagan reached for, and it's what Henry Kissinger buried.
paragraphs.zh: 帝国的教义是掠夺、落后和永无止境的战争。而美国的回答则是建设、制造、创新和开发。这就是美国体系。艾森豪威尔秉持它，威廉·罗杰斯为之奋斗，里根努力追求它，而亨利·基辛格却将其埋葬。

paragraphs.en: And now Donald [clears throat] Trump is bringing it back. Remember the word the vice president left us with. The highest honor history can bestow is peacemaker. Well, they handed Henry Kissinger a Nobel Peace Prize and he spent his entire life managing wars so they would never end.
paragraphs.zh: 如今，唐纳德（清嗓子）特朗普正将其重新带回。还记得副总统留给我们的那句话吗？历史所能赋予的最高荣誉是“和平缔造者”。然而，他们把诺贝尔和平奖颁给了亨利·基辛格，而他的一生都在运筹帷幄，让战争永无终结之日。

paragraphs.en: But the real peacemaker isn't the man who balances terror. It's the man who ends it. And that's what Nixon threatened and that's what Trump is delivering. And that's why in both cases they have to be stopped.
paragraphs.zh: 但真正的和平缔造者并非那个以恐怖平衡维持局面的人，而是那个终结恐怖的人。尼克松曾以此相威胁，而特朗普正在付诸实施。正因如此，在这两种情况下，他们都必须被制止。

paragraphs.en: And that the end of the doctrine that took him down is Nixon's revenge. So, when you watch the day-to-day in the Middle East, remember what you're really looking at. Not a ceasefire or an agreement or a memorandum, but the dismantling of a century-old British war machine, one country and one strategic doctrine at a time. This is the work of a peacemaker, the title history honors the most, and the one empire always fears.
paragraphs.zh: 而终结其政治生涯的，正是那套曾将他拉下马的教条的反噬——尼克松的复仇。因此，当你审视中东的日常局势时，请记住你所真正目睹的：并非停火协议、外交协定或备忘录，而是一个世纪以来英国战争机器的逐步瓦解，以一个个国家、一项项战略教条为单元，逐一拆解。这才是真正和解者的工作，是历史赋予最高荣誉的称谓，也是任何帝国始终深怀恐惧的存在。

paragraphs.en: Promethean Action is committed to defending the peacemakers from the empire, and that fight is now coming to a head. So, join us by becoming a supporting member or a contributor. This has been your Monday brief. Thanks for watching. >> Please take a moment and subscribe to our free newsletter at prometheanaction.com.
paragraphs.zh: 普罗米修斯行动致力于保护和平缔造者免受帝国的侵害，而这场斗争如今已到了关键时刻。因此，请通过成为支持会员或捐助者来加入我们。以上就是本周一的简报。感谢收看。>> 请花一点时间订阅我们在 prometheanaction.com 上的免费通讯。
